programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps a single mum whose cat, Pickles, is terrifying her children. He also treats Lucifer, a cat whose food obsession may cost him his life

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy helps a veteran with PTSD whose therapy cat is making him feel worse. He also deals with a feline that is tearing up its owners furniture and their marriage

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Documentary following the Texan wardens as deer poacher is busted, a baby rattlesnake is rescued, a hummingbird evades capture and a raccoon is released back into the wild

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff performs a risky operation to save a puppys leg - but whether the pooch will walk again is another matter. Plus, an abandoned cats x-ray tells a shocking story

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson heads to Alabamas Appalachia to build a treehouse fit for a budding James Bond, featuring talking gadgets, hidden passageways, secret rooms and more

programme.title: The Pool Master
programme.desc: Anthony Archer-Wills and his team rip out a boring backyard and replace it with a Japanese-style water garden, complete with a mountain hot tub and multiple waterfalls

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: The Kilchers pay tribute to the family heads, Yule and Ruth, by preparing a feast using strictly old-fashioned methods

programme.title: Alaska Gold Diggers
programme.desc: The team is on a roll at Gold Run, and things are beginning to look up. But everyone is forced to evacuate the camp when freezing winter storms approach

programme.title: Beasts of the Bayou
programme.desc: Fishermen search for the rougarou, a Cajun werewolf thought to be responsible for a recent surge in creature sightings

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: Expert homesteader Marty Raney and his daughter Misty help Montana residents Beth and Mike, who are stalked by a mountain lion, and are struggling to find food, water or livestock

programme.title: Ninja Sharks
programme.desc: When fish huddle together for protection, a hungry shark needs to get creative. The Thresher Shark knows just the trick

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Out on a Limb
programme.desc: Pete Nelson is called upon to design a bear observation treehouse for two sisters on their 1,000-acre property and creates his first-ever mid-century decagon house with glass walls

programme.title: Animal Cops San Francisco
programme.desc: Officer Forester races to save the life of a critically ill cat, Lieutenant Scott tracks down a dog that ran away following an explosion and the team tries to rescue 77 collies

programme.title: Animal Cops San Francisco
programme.desc: Officer Sadler discovers what she believes to be fighting cockerels in the back of a pick-up truck, and Lieutenant Scott faces a daring bridge rescue

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the fauna of Africa, home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth. But even in this land of plenty, animals face huge challenges. At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods

programme.title: Travels with Pevsner
programme.desc: Germaine Greer explores the architecture of Warwickshire with Nikolaus Pevsners Buildings of England as her guide, with visits to Baddesley Clinton Hall and Warwick Castle

programme.title: Rebuilding Notre-Dame
programme.desc: On December 7, 2024, the cathedral of Notre-Dame de Paris reopened five years after the devastating fire that ravaged the world-famous gothic masterpiece. Here, Lucy Worsley follows the final stages of the restoration work, focusing on the teams battling to ensure the complex roof and spire were ready for the public reopening

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the fauna of Africa, home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth. But even in this land of plenty, animals face huge challenges. At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods

programme.title: The Latest Secrets of Hieroglyphs
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the latest research into the creation of the ancient Egyptians written language. The programme follows Egyptologists as they unlock the texts inscribed inside a richly adorned tomb, revealing the beliefs and lives of the priests, scribes, painters, engravers and builders who created this grand funerary monument

programme.title: Call My Bluff
programme.desc: Robert Robinson hosts as Patrick Campbell, Joanna Lumley and Frank Kearton take on Frank Muir, Pauline Yates and Donald Churchill in the game of word definitions and deceptions. First broadcast in 1976

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against guests Annette Page, Richard Baker and Robin Ray. With guest musician Murray Perahia. First broadcast in 1979

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin and the team return, beginning by exploring the progress of Nasas Artemis II mission, the first crewed journey back toward the Moon in more than 50 years. It is designed to carry astronauts around the far side and safely home again, marking the furthest human beings will have travelled into space.

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: How discoveries from the edge of the solar system transformed what was known about Pluto, thanks to the New Horizons space probe that took the first-ever close-up images of the dwarf planet. Pluto was once thought to be geologically dead, but the pictures revealed it to be an active world of stunning complexity, with mountains carved from ice, a nitrogen glacier that appeared to be moving and a recently active volcano. The data even led some scientists to speculate there may even be life on Pluto today

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: Contemporary artists and performers, alongside historians and curators,examine the creative renewal in literature, music and art in the aftermath of the Black Death, with survivors finding their voice, questioning authority and challenging status and class. Above all, writing in English was revived by works including Geoffrey Chaucers The Canterbury Tales, William Langlands satire The Vision of Piers Plowman and breakthrough works by women like the spirited pilgrim Margery Kempe

programme.title: The Secret Life of Waves
programme.desc: David Malone investigates the importance of ocean waves. He discovers they are not made of water, can travel sideways and their sound is created by bubbles, and examines the connection between the life cycles of waves and human beings

programme.title: The Latest Secrets of Hieroglyphs
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the latest research into the creation of the ancient Egyptians written language. The programme follows Egyptologists as they unlock the texts inscribed inside a richly adorned tomb, revealing the beliefs and lives of the priests, scribes, painters, engravers and builders who created this grand funerary monument

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: Contemporary artists and performers, alongside historians and curators,examine the creative renewal in literature, music and art in the aftermath of the Black Death, with survivors finding their voice, questioning authority and challenging status and class. Above all, writing in English was revived by works including Geoffrey Chaucers The Canterbury Tales, William Langlands satire The Vision of Piers Plowman and breakthrough works by women like the spirited pilgrim Margery Kempe

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Industrial Age
programme.desc: A tour of Britains mining heritage takes Fred from Cornwall to Scotland, where he visits sites of lead, slate, tin and coal extraction. Along the way, the machine-mad former steeplejack has a chance to man a winding engine and descends a mine shaft while strapped to the top of the cage

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming travels along the Rochdale Canal from Sowerby Bridge to Manchester. But disaster strikes when he runs aground, threatening to stop him from reaching his final destination

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth plans a candlelit supper to impress Emmet, the director of the local amateur operatic society, with her singing talents. But fate intervenes and the event does not go as expected. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and David Griffin

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy makes plans to seduce Veronica at his bring-a-bottle party, though he must first get his parents to go on a second honeymoon. Ronnie Corbett stars

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: The feud between Queen Elizabeth I, a resourceful and ruthless monarch who had the difficult task of ruling a Protestant state in a climate still strongly influenced by Catholicism and her cousin Mary, Queen of Scots, whose conspiring ultimately led to her execution. Simon Schama questions whether Elizabeths eventual victory over her rival rang hollow, given that Mary was mother to the main contender for the throne

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The first in a two-part documentary exploring the divisive ideological conflicts reshaping higher education in the western world. With secret recordings, insider testimony, and rare intimate access to professors, administrators and students, this programme paints a vivid portrait of a system under pressure

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin and the team return, beginning by exploring the progress of Nasas Artemis II mission, the first crewed journey back toward the Moon in more than 50 years. It is designed to carry astronauts around the far side and safely home again, marking the furthest human beings will have travelled into space.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming concludes his journey by travelling from Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool Docks - but he experiences engine issues and a leaky boat along the way

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred visits the North East, a region rich in transport history, to explore the development of Britains railways, from the first steam locomotives to the record-breaking Mallard. A visit to Yorks National Railway Museum offers the chance to ride a replica of Stephensons Rocket, while at Ffestiniog Railway he learns about the computer-aided design process used for new locomotives

programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Cows Stranded in a River, Poachers Tracked by Drone
programme.desc: In Co Down, dairy farmer Derek Geddis turns to a volunteer search and rescue team after three of his cattle go missing. When the animals are found stranded in a river, the farmers skills and the teams technology are put to the test in a delicate recovery mission. In Co Durham, a community support officers drone footage helps catch deer poachers in the act. His quick response and aerial evidence lead to a rare conviction. Plus, in north Wales, reporter Paul Colgan investigates how police and scientists are using dog DNA to trace the animals behind devastating livestock attacks

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: In Harthill, Scotland, Martel Maxwell encounters a house with a safety order attached to it, which means certain rooms cannot be accessed. Owain Wyn Evans heads to south Wales to see a sizeable unit with lots of development potential. Meanwhile, in Lincolnshire, Dion Dublin does all he can to escape the rain

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of sisters compete at an antiques centre in Derbyshire, aided by Nick Hall and Izzie Balmer. Plus, Christina Trevanion meets a man who built his own cinema in his garden

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: Joanna Page presents a shopping challenge, where couples scour the UKs charity shops to see if they can make even more money at auction for the items they purchase, and raise money for their chosen causes. In Hastings, partners James and Elaine scour 15 charity shops, hunting for vintage gems and a hidden star buy before facing a tense, live online auction for charity

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Interior designer Banjo Beale and his team of tradespeople transforms a family-run fish shop in Tobermory on the Isle of Mull. The job is a commission from Banjos friend Sally, who is the manager and wants to modernise the traditional business

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Richie Anderson helps a couple fulfil their forest school dream in Oxfordshire. He also meets an innovative artist who creates masterpieces on a bicycle-powered sewing machine

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea revisits upholsterer Hayley and stone carver Richard to find out how they have built money-making businesses after leaving his business bootcamp

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which Lee, Nadine, Tom, Carol and Albert race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Tom Allen for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ross and Vicki celebrate their stag and hen parties, Jasmine starts to feel part of the Slater family and Bea makes a decision about her love life

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan completes this leg of his journey along Irelands Wild Atlantic in County Derry/Londonderry. He walks the 400-year-old walls of the city of Derry/Londonderry with comedian Diona Doherty. He also hits the farm to find out what it takes to be a competitive sheep herder, before heading for the windswept coastline of Castlerock to meet a skilled craft worker who builds traditional coracle boats by hand

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: TV presenter and keen gardener Katie Piper joins Diarmuid and Carol to visit three more gardens. The judges head to Colins surprising rustic garden in suburban Ballymena, before seeing how Adrienne has transformed two acres near the windswept Glenshane Pass in the foothills of the Sperrin Mountains. Their final stop is in Dromore, Co Down, where Julietta has created a garden designed with her young family in mind, including a wild meadow playground and a courtyard filled with edible plants

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Lisa tries to find a way of getting Stephen to talk to her. Amanda splits up with Dave before beginning the move to her new life

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local News from across Northern Ireland with Tara Mills and Declan Harvey

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Darragh MacIntyre reports on Donald Trumps hardline immigration policies, which have left an estimated 14 million undocumented immigrants are at risk of deportation and resulted in a sharp rise in arrests in US cities and protesters being shot dead on the streets of Minneapolis by federal agents

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Monty Don hosts, with guest panellists comedian Chris McCausland and journalist Helen Lewis joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the latest news

programme.title: Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy (2011)
programme.desc: George Smiley, the deputy to the head of British intelligence, is forced into retirement after an operation ends in disaster with the death of an MI6 agent. Smiley is later recruited by the government to return to duty and investigate the possibility of a Soviet mole in a high-ranking position within MI6. Cold War thriller based on John le Carres 1974 novel, starring an Oscar-nominated Gary Oldman, alongside Colin Firth, Tom Hardy, John Hurt, Benedict Cumberbatch, Toby Jones, Mark Strong, Ciaran Hinds and Roger Lloyd Pack

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: A young patient is reported to have had a seizure while out walking in a forest in Bingley, West Yorkshire - but paramedics Sam and Sophie are struggling to locate her. Meanwhile, crewmates Jordan and Josh are on their way to an 85-year-old woman with chest pains when they are diverted to a category one call for an unconscious male. However, they quickly realise the man in question is conscious and a regular caller to 999 who would be best seen at hospital - an offer he refuses

programme.title: Morning Live

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Dead Birds of Prey Discovery Reveals Shocking Crime
programme.desc: On farmland in Wiltshire, investigators uncover one of Britains most shocking cases of bird crime, after a tip-off leads to a secret camera catching a suspect in the act. As teams sift through horrific evidence of dead raptors, the scale of what could be the UKs largest bird of prey persecution becomes clear. In Northern Ireland, reporter Paul Colgan joins the Mourne Mountain Rescue Team to discover the challenges they overcome to keep hill farmers safe in some of the countrys toughest terrain

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts encounters a layout he has never seen before, which is rare given his 20-plus years on the show. Dion Dublin encounters all weathers in Manchester and Leeds

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from Shropshires Oswestry Showground, and travels to north Wales to meet sculptor Simon ORourke. With Charles Hanson and Ben Cooper

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: Joanna Page is in High Wycombe, Buckinghamshire, where mother and daughter Liz and Alice compete to raise money for Great Ormond Street Hospital. With just £100 each and 60 minutes on the clock, they scour local charity shops, taking a gamble on vintage fashion, ceramics and the bold purchase of a sewing machine

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Interior designer Banjo Beale and his team of tradespeople update a crumbling bothy on the small island of Ulva, which locals want to rent out to visitors. With no power, water or easy access, Banjo has his work cut out, but the help of handyman Tom, painter Lisa, and his husband Ro enable him to embark on his most challenging design brief to date

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple who have never lived in an older home, but who are now ready to fall for a bit of period charm in either Norfolk or Suffolk. With a £270,000 budget and three lively whippets to please, they are after a dog-friendly garden and a slice of East Anglian architectural history to call home. Alistair also meets a willow farmer and discovers how this wonderfully sustainable crop is being woven into the future

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea revisits metal sculptor Lindon and keepsake artist Andie to find out about their growing businesses. Lindon came to Doms bootcamp looking for help to build a dream family business in the summer of 2023. In Devon, Andie reveals how Dom and mentor Sonny Bailey-Airds support and guidance have helped her

programme.title: The Finish Line

The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Tom Allen host the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, presented by Alex Jones and Roman Kemp

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bea grows jealous of Honey, Vicky finds herself in an escalating situation and Oscar tries to avoid Josh

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and the team head to Yorkshire to help the Mann-Monro family, a six-strong household overwhelmed by years of clutter and emotional memories. Mum of three Mary met stepdad Stuart 10 years ago, but after almost a decade of living together, during which time they welcomed fourth child Rosie, their house is now bursting at the seams. But among the things to sort are items belonging to Marys late husband - things she packed away years ago but never confronted. Now the children have the chance to learn more about their father

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Lisa and Stephen navigate their new pregnancy. Dave tries to connect with his son. Drama, starring Paapa Essiedu and Siobhán Cullen

programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sketch show set in Belfast, where the cats get stoned, the pigeons get philosophical and Gambo forgets how to breathe

programme.title: Yesterday (2019)
programme.desc: Jack, a struggling musician, gets hit by a bus during a mysterious event. When he regains consciousness, he finds that he is the only person who can remember the Beatles, and sets out to exploit the opportunity by passing their hits off as his own compositions. Danny Boyles comedy, starring Himesh Patel and Lily James

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the comedy quiz show in which a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he is joined by Richard Ayoade, Alex Jones, Stephen Bailey, Nadia Jae, Gyles Brandreth and Roisin Conaty

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: The case of Michaella McCollum, a teenager from Northern Ireland who formed a relationship with a drug trafficker during her first trip abroad in Ibiza. He persuaded her to assist him in smuggling drugs for his cartel contacts, and just a few weeks later, she was arrested at Lima airport in Peru with more than a million pounds worth of cocaine in her suitcase

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Young offender Michaella McCollum, who became infamous worldwide as one half of the Peru Two, offers her account of how she came to be arrested, tried and jailed in one of the most notorious maximum security prisons in Peru. She recounts how she and fellow would-be mule Melissa Reid were tasked with pretending to be tourists at Machu Picchu, before walking 12kg of cocaine into Jorge Chávez International Airport in August 2013

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tir Dhondy goes further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting locals

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. After a video featuring a female prison officer and an inmate having sex inside HMP Wandsworth goes viral, journalist Mobeen Azhar begins an investigation into what happened and why. A year of twists, turns and damning revelations pulls back the curtain on a dangerous wider world of coercion, corruption and contraband thriving behind bars

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mobeen Azhars investigation into prison corruption uncovers the murder of a former officer who had been involved in exposing an illicit relationship. When news breaks of a prison governor being jailed for a relationship with a drug-dealing gang boss, Mobeen becomes determined to uncover the true extent of criminal influence inside our prisons

programme.title: Press X to Continue
programme.desc: Steffan Powell chats with Elz the Witch, Inel Tomlinson and Glenn Moore about which eight games they would take to a mythical desert island

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores the world of parasites, including tapeworms, mosquitoes and cuckoos, investigating the skills they use to exploit their hosts

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores dangerous wildlife in Venezuela, including green anacondas, vampire bats and scolopendras - giant venomous centipedes

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May try to impress Oxford residents by building an electric car, and refuse to let an unenthusiastic response deter them from sitting in on the machines crash-test before handing it over to the Stigs environmentally friendly cousin. Actor Michael Sheen is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, and Jeremy pits the Corvette ZR1 against the Audi R8 V10

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: The intrepid reporter explores different aspects of Los Angeles, beginning by meeting the characters whose lives revolve around the citys neglected and feral dogs. He goes on patrol with a man on a mission to rescue some of the thousands of stray and ferocious pit bulls roaming the streets and meets hard-pressed staff at LAs largest animal shelter. Louis also encounters training gurus who claim to be able to take the ghetto out of the dog once it has been removed from that environment

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: Louis explores the American attitude to death as he visits Cedars-Sinai Medical Centre in West Hollywood, where he meets people battling against serious illnesses. He talks to patients and their families as they face whether to accept death and die in relative comfort, or to gamble on further, possibly painful treatments to prolong their lives, and meets the doctors whose job it is to guide people through the traumatic process

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: California has some of the toughest regulations controlling sex offenders anywhere in America - after leaving prison they can spend years on parole being tagged and tracked and a lifetime on an online register. In his final film exploring different aspects of life in Los Angeles, Louis Theroux visits hostels to talk to parolees as well as the people charged with monitoring them, and asks whether the restrictions keep society safer and when, if ever, it is right to give sex offenders a second chance

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Michaella explains she was faced with a dilemma - stick to her story about being forced to carry the drugs and face 15 years in jail, or come clean and break her mothers heart

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Michaella discusses how she claimed to have been forced into smuggling in the hope of getting a reduced sentence, only to find herself spending the first of what would be many days behind bars in the cells of the polices anti-narcotics division. However, her story soon caught the attention of the international press, with reporters around the world desperate for the inside scoop

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: Louis explores the American attitude to death as he visits Cedars-Sinai Medical Centre in West Hollywood, where he meets people battling against serious illnesses. He talks to patients and their families as they face whether to accept death and die in relative comfort, or to gamble on further, possibly painful treatments to prolong their lives, and meets the doctors whose job it is to guide people through the traumatic process

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: California has some of the toughest regulations controlling sex offenders anywhere in America - after leaving prison they can spend years on parole being tagged and tracked and a lifetime on an online register. In his final film exploring different aspects of life in Los Angeles, Louis Theroux visits hostels to talk to parolees as well as the people charged with monitoring them, and asks whether the restrictions keep society safer and when, if ever, it is right to give sex offenders a second chance

programme.title: Press X to Continue
programme.desc: Steffan Powell is joined by SunPi, Mr Midas and Shay Thompson to talk through whats going on with online game stores at Xbox, Playstation and Nintendo

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall returns to South America to search for more of the planets deadliest animals. He joins a team trying to save a highly endangered predator, the Orinoco crocodile and witnesses the ferocious maternal instincts of a female croc protecting her nest. In Brazil he heads out for a night-time encounter with a metre long monster of the Amazonian waterways, the wolf fish and back on dry land Steve turns to technology in an attempt to track down one of South Americas most elusive jungle predators, the enigmatic Jaguar

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the crew head to Sri Lanka in search of dangerous predators, hunting for blue whales and tracking down Asian elephants. Along the way they encounter two deadly snakes, a venomous Russells viper and the enormous Indian rock python

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Leaving the tranquillity of Kefalonia behind, the teams race through Greece to reach one of Europes biggest and most vibrant cities, Istanbul, where Europe and Asia collide. As ever, money is tight and just one missed connection can mean disaster. Without mobile phones to help them, the teams have to rely on their wits to make it to the end of the second stage. John Hannah narrates

programme.title: Muslim Matchmaker
programme.desc: Bucky, Uneeb and Yasmine hit dating hurdles, while Hoda takes on new client Imran

programme.title: Muslim Matchmaker
programme.desc: A new match for Yasmine, decision day for Imran, and Bucky learns the truth about Rehan

programme.title: Respect (2021)
programme.desc: Biopic of Aretha Franklin, following the first three decades of her life, from being born as a musical prodigy in an affluent African-American family, the repercussions of losing her mother at age 10, to her arduous rise to international musical stardom, while enduring an abusive marriage, ultimately concluding with the recording of her influential live album Amazing Grace. Starring Jennifer Hudson and Forest Whitaker

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Frances second-largest city Marseille has been the nations drug capital for over 50 years, but the violence of its drug war is escalating, as rival gangs kidnap, torture, kill and even set one another on fire. Ashley spends time in the notorious northern suburbs known as the cites, meeting young men involved in fighting the bloody drug war as well as riding along with the police on a night-time patrol

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: Despite being sentenced to nearly seven years in a maximum security prison, Michaella was home in less than three. She reveals how she got out early and how the experience changed her for the better

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May try to impress Oxford residents by building an electric car, and refuse to let an unenthusiastic response deter them from sitting in on the machines crash-test before handing it over to the Stigs environmentally friendly cousin. Actor Michael Sheen is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, and Jeremy pits the Corvette ZR1 against the Audi R8 V10

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sketch comedy following the residents of a quiet suburban street that quickly becomes a playground for comic mayhem-absurd, awkward, and unexpectedly hilarious

programme.title: Louis Therouxs LA Stories
programme.desc: The intrepid reporter explores different aspects of Los Angeles, beginning by meeting the characters whose lives revolve around the citys neglected and feral dogs. He goes on patrol with a man on a mission to rescue some of the thousands of stray and ferocious pit bulls roaming the streets and meets hard-pressed staff at LAs largest animal shelter. Louis also encounters training gurus who claim to be able to take the ghetto out of the dog once it has been removed from that environment

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian visits Uganda to find out whether Brits should add this country to their holiday bucket list. He is hosted by Bugandan princess Alex, who teaches him about Ugandas history before and after dictator Idi Amins rule

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Flashbacks show Stephen and Lisas first meeting, while Amandas pregnancy and Daves ambitions create uncertainty for all

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: At a friends wedding, Lisa struggles in the face of Amandas pregnancy, while Stephen tries to find the courage to confront Dave. Drama starring Paapa Essiedu and Siobhán Cullen as a couple dealing with the aftermath of pregnancy loss, with Charlotte Riley and Jack Bannon

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents a compilation edition focusing on motoring memorabilia, with items including a vintage stop sign that brought an Oswestry team to an abrupt halt and a duo in Essex who used magic to drive down the price of a luxury car mascot. Eric journeys along the Time Road exhibition at the British Motor Museum and in the Cotswolds, Christina Trevanion goes for a spin at one of Britains most historic racing circuits

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the hidden wild world of Britains backyards. The first episode focuses on the garden of a thousand-year-old mill house in Oxfordshire. As the winter floods recede, a kingfisher searches for a mate, a vole must evade a range of dangers, and otters and red kites patrol the river, both day and night

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Chelsea v Manchester City at Stamford Bridge and Nottingham Forest v Aston Villa at The City Ground. City were unbeaten in their last 12 matches with Chelsea in all competitions, but the sides drew 1-1 in the reverse fixture at Etihad Stadium in January. Forest and the Villans were both in needs of points for differing reasons, with the hosts battling to avoid relegation and Unai Emerys men aiming to qualify for the Champions League. Plus, Crystal Palace v Newcastle United and Sunderland v Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clive Myries Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: In Jamaicas capital city, Clive discovers a new hidden talent when he tries out some dancehall moves. He explores a 19th-century mansion, which tells the story of Jamaicas first black millionaire. Clive heads to the school that has produced most of the countrys Olympic sprinting talent and samples a Jamaican-Irish stout punch

programme.title: Natures Epic Journeys
programme.desc: Documentary following 100,000 caribou as they face bears, wolves, frozen rivers and rugged mountains to undertake the worlds longest land migration. Liz Bonnin and a team of scientists track the herd as they travel 5,000km through the Arctic wilderness of Canada and Alaska, revealing insights into the lives of individual animals as they attempt the epic journey. Cameras capture the caribou as they fight to reach their calving grounds before they give birth - failure would spell disaster

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The experts gather in Hartlepool, where they evaluate a Steiff teddy bear bought for a dog to play with, and a large Chinese paper passport that helped to save lives. Other items include a piece of shrapnel from the 1914 bombardment of the town and a 17th-century jar with possible ties to Oliver Cromwell

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Thomas Plant and Adam Partridge look out for potential treasures at Chiddingstone Castle in Kent, while presenter Paul Martin visits Chartwell House, home to Winston Churchill, to uncover the real man behind the political persona

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph, Ben Thompson, Laura Tobin and Henning Wehn test their general knowledge skills in a variety of fun trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Japan for a trip through the country, beginning his first leg in its second city Osaka, which is famous for its food culture, nightlife and friendly people. He then takes a ride on a Shinkansen bullet train and end in Nagoya, Japans manufacturing hub

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Investigations from BBC correspondents all over the world, revealing secrets, rooting out injustice and exposing crime, corruption and abuse

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: The comedian matches wildlife sanctuary owner Lindsay with celebrity artist and animal lover Ricky Wilson, but the Kaiser Chiefs frontman faces an artistic dilemma. Lindsay wants the portrait to focus on the animals she has dedicated her life to, but Ricky believes it is Lindsay who is extraordinary and should be at the centre of the painting

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie hosts the concluding semi-final of the quiz, with four returning contenders answering questions on the specialist subjects of Amelia Earhart, Laurence Olivier, the red wines of Burgundy, and the songs of Noël Coward. They are then each tested on general knowledge as they try to win the remaining place in the grand final

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Manchester take on Imperial College London in the concluding semi-final, hoping to take one step closer to lifting the series trophy. Amol Rajan asks the questions

programme.title: Miriam Margolyes Made Me Me
programme.desc: Director Simon Draper set out to make a podcast with actress Miriam Margolyes, but the project soon became sidetracked as he was drawn ever deeper into her orbit. The podcast turned into a chaotic, joyful road trip, revealing Miriams true legacy isnt just her work, but the lives she has quietly transformed, beginning with the film-maker himself

programme.title: Éadaí SOS
programme.desc: Caoimhe Ní Chathail and Proinsias Ó Coinn return to challenge more fashionistas to alter their shopping habits, supporting them in reducing, reusing and recycling their clothes. in the opening edition, Caoimhe visits Clodagh, who loves to shop, especially for handbags. Can she be inspired to wear the clothes she already owns and find out more about sustainable handbag options?

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: nsights and global interviews on the news stories of the day, presented by Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Charmain and the Prophet
programme.desc: Reporter Myles Bonnar investigates the tragedy that befell Arbroath single mum Charmain Speirs after she married Ghanaian preacher Eric Adusah after a whirlwind romance. Less than six months later, Charmain was found dead in a hotel in Ghana. Meeting Charmains family and friends, and those close to the case, Myles asks what happened in Room 112 and investigates the truth behind Adusah, known as the Prophet

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer head to Norfolk on the hunt for tench at Rocklands Mere, a hidden gem in the quaint village of Rocklands St Peter. After a pretty perfect first day at the lakes, they decide to reward themselves with a pint in the Brisley Bell pub, where Bob reveals they are staying overnight, much to Pauls delight. Later, Dr Anand Patel pays them a visit by the lake

programme.title: The Chronicles of Strangford
programme.desc: It is Easter and in Portaferry the plants on the tea plantation are taken out of the poly tunnel. Colin Morgan narrates the documentary

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Its week 11, and the final five candidates face a gruelling round of interviews with some of Lord Sugars toughest-talking business associates. As their business plans are picked apart, some contestants hold their own, while others crumble under the pressure. In the boardroom, Alan Sugar has a tough decision to make, as only two can make it through to next weeks grand final

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: Easter: The Sights and Sounds of Spring
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit Bisterne Estate in Hampshires Avon Valley on Easter Sunday, where farmland and water meadows provide a vital home for lapwings and hares. Plus, author Robert Macfarlane shares an exclusive spring reading, and on his Cotswolds farm, Adam Henson restores a traditional hedgerow, just in time for nesting birds

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and the team head to Twickenham to help the Allan family tackle their overflowing home. Mum Sharon has ADHD and thrives when things have a place - but standards have slipped and the house has never had a proper clear-out. Daughter Holly, who is autistic, finds comfort in routine and familiar objects and feels overwhelmed by the clutter. As the team snoop around, they soon discover part of the problem - dad Brians secret gadget-buying habit

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: Ian Fletcher starts a new chapter in Miami as director of integrity for the Fifa World Cup, overseeing the biggest football event in the world. His first challenge is to lead the Oversight Team towards a decision on which cities should host the semi-final matches. Spoof documentary sequel, starring Hugh Bonneville

programme.sub-title: Inferno in the Fields and the Fight to Save Badgers
programme.desc: Police close in on an organised crime gang targeting farms and stealing high-tech GPS systems worth hundreds of thousands of pounds at farms across the north of England. In Leicestershire, a huge barn fire devastates on a family run dairy farm, threatening cattle and destroying vital winter feed. As the flames spread, farmer Blaise and daughter Millie race to save their livestock. In Scotland, reporter Paul Colgan meets farmer Debbie Duffus, as she is reunited with the air ambulance team members who saved her life after a brutal farm accident

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The freedivers search for a treasured possession lost in a boat fire 17 years ago, and social media detective Mui finds a hidden clue in a drone

programme.title: Clive Myries Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: In Downtown Kingston, Clive brushes up on his Jamaican patois and tries out some popular street food. He catches up with a mural art collective, who have been transforming this district. Clive also checks out a boutique where he gets kitted out with some brand new Downtown threads to look the part for a night out at a reggae club

programme.title: Natures Epic Journeys
programme.desc: Documentary following thousands of zebra as they face lions, hunting dogs, drought and starvation to undertake the worlds most newly discovered migration. Braving one of Botswanas most brutal wildernesses along the way, the animals make Africas longest land migration to reach the grasslands of Nxai Pan National Park. Liz Bonnin and a team of scientists and filmmakers use cutting-edge technology to capture the zebra every step of the way, and reveal the secrets behind the brand new finding

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A day at Newmarket racecourse in Suffolk brings out equine-themed valuables, including a bronze of the racehorse Ibrahim, which has retained its worth despite a wonky ear caused by a fall. An inscribed loving-cup also comes under scrutiny, along with a pearlware horse which holds a few surprises for the owner

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Adam Partridge and James Lewis value items in the North Yorkshire town of Richmond, including a gold pocket watch and a collection of pipes. Presenter Paul Martin also explores the process of making Wensleydale cheese

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph, Ben Thompson, Laura Tobin and Henning Wehn take on a series of quiz rounds selected to test their general knowledge skills

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to the cultural heart of Japan, Kyoto, the city that for a thousand years was the capital. At the Rozanji Temple, he learns about a book that is described as the worlds first novel, the Tale of Genji. At Kyotos Matsunoo shrine, Michael gets a beginners guide to shrine etiquette and at the end of the day he takes a trip to a local restaurant to meet Satoha, an apprentice geisha

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: South West England: Starter and Fish
programme.desc: Four chefs from south west England cook canapes, starters and fish dishes and their creations are judged by Michelin-starred chef Simon Rogan. Dishes include a potato peel pie that pays homage to the film The Guernsey Literary and Potato Peel Pie Society. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Britains Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The captain of HMS Queen Elizabeth deliberately seeks out the worst possible weather so he can test how the F35 jets cope under extreme conditions - and gets his wish when the aircraft carrier ends up in the path of a hurricane. After consulting with his officers, he resolves to brave the elements and test the jets to the brink of destruction. The crew also faces the challenge of refueling the vessel at sea - one of the hardest tasks outside of going into battle

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Rhod Gilbert, Ignacio Lopez and Zoe Lyons join regular panellist Alan Davies on the comedy quiz, with host Sandi Toksvig asking questions on the theme of viral

programme.title: The Chronicles of Strangford
programme.desc: Its autumn at Strangford Lough. Songwriter Brigid looks for inspiration on the banks of the lough, sailor Kenny prepares his boat for the winter

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: TV presenter and keen gardener Katie Piper joins Diarmuid and Carol to visit three more gardens. The judges head to Colins surprising rustic garden in suburban Ballymena, before seeing how Adrienne has transformed two acres near the windswept Glenshane Pass in the foothills of the Sperrin Mountains. Their final stop is in Dromore, Co Down, where Julietta has created a garden designed with her young family in mind, including a wild meadow playground and a courtyard filled with edible plants

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Leaving the tranquillity of Kefalonia behind, the teams race through Greece to reach one of Europes biggest and most vibrant cities, Istanbul, where Europe and Asia collide. As ever, money is tight and just one missed connection can mean disaster. Without mobile phones to help them, the teams have to rely on their wits to make it to the end of the second stage. John Hannah narrates

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Egypts Unexplained Files
programme.desc: An exploration of crime and punishment in ancient Egypt. The topic features homicidal royalty, rampant tomb raiding and acts of extreme punishment

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Orb and Crash Debris, KGB UFOs and Aliens and Superhuman Reflexes
programme.desc: The notion that a silver orb spotted in the sky and alleged crash debris may be from a UFO. Plus, a 65-year-old-man with the fastest hands in the West and an infamous spy organisations alleged footage of UFOs and aliens

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Beastly Howls, Hollowman Air Force Base UFO, and the Cabin That Defies Gravity
programme.desc: A photograph of a UFO hovering over Holloman Air Force Base in New Mexico and a cabin built in a supposedly anomalous spot that defies gravity in the Redwood Forests. Plus, the origin of frightening howls recorded in Witset, British Columbia

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Worm in the Sky, Skeletal Mystery Remains and Thessaloniki Mystery Sounds
programme.desc: A remotely operated vehicle (ROV) finds the remains of a massive skeleton 825 metres below the surface of the Mediterranean. Plus, a building that appears to shrink as a family gets closer to it and a strange worm-shaped object spotted in the sky

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts is in Glasgow to investigate the impact of steam power on Britains major towns in the 18th and 19th centuries. She also learns about the arrival of migrant workers in the city and how the insanitary conditions in which they lived, which made Glasgow both a hotbed of disease and a place of great medical advancement. Aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson flies his drone over Loch Katrine as he tells the story of how Victorian engineers overcame geographical barriers to bring fresh drinking water to the workers of Glasgow

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores Edinburgh and its role in the union between England and Scotland. She discovers how the famous case of Burke and Hares bodysnatching influenced British medical law, and visits the Royal Bank of Scotland, where she uncovers evidence of the worlds first overdraft. Plus, the role played by Henry Dundas, whose statue stands in St Andrews Square, in the continuation of the slave trade

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team travel to Chesterfield, Derbyshire, to value antiques including Moorcroft plates, a poster of the Who and a pair of Marley horses. Plus, dealer Michael Hogben looks at a gold and diamond fob watch

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick tackles a football helmet signed by 28 Heisman Trophy winners, while Corey gears up to buy a devastatingly cool Transformers toy for the shop. Rick tries not to go psycho over an Alfred Hitchcock poster that makes a cameo in the shop

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly: Henrys Wreck-trospective
programme.desc: Compilation of episodes from the show Junk and Disorderly, which features Henry Cole and other restorers buying, fixing, and selling automotive junk

programme.title: Storage Wars

programme.desc: The chef takes on one of his toughest challenges yet, J Willys Bar and Grille in South Bend, Indiana, which has amassed debts of more than $1million. programme.desc: Three couples from Wirral on Merseyside compete, beginning with Sandra and her husband Graham who met in the Army more than 17 years earlier. programme.desc: George heads to Kent to help Pete and Amelie make their huge 16 room Victorian house fit for modern life, with plans to make a playroom and a library. Amelie makes a trip to Shropshire to make her own encaustic tiles in one of the last remaining factories in the country to use traditional methods. Plus, George revisits Michele in Warwickshire to see how she is getting on remodelling her adjoining Grade II listed cottages

programme.desc: Sam and Diane grow tired of Coach hanging around them all the time, so they set him up on a date. Starring Ted Danson and Shelley Long

programme.desc: Cliffs pedantic know-it-all attitude finally gets him into trouble with another patron and it is not long before his bravery is called into question. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and John Ratzenberger

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Coach acquires an antique weighing machine that also gives out fortunes - which start coming true in eerie fashion - but Diane brushes off the predictions as coincidence, until she is forced to revise her opinion

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: The Barones reminisce about more peaceful times in their first apartment and recall why they ended up living across the road from Rays meddlesome parents

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Rays relatives reluctantly tell him the brutal truth about his performance as a panellist on Roy Firestones live TV sports show. American comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Seattle shrinks agent Bebe puts her lowly assistant in charge of his career when a popular new radio psychiatrist appears on the scene. Meanwhile, Martin seems unwilling to make a doctors appointment. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Roz tries everyones patience by talking incessantly about her new love Roger, and Frasier offers to help protect Martin from a bullying boss. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part one of two. Peace and harmony are shattered by the arrival of Daphnes conniving mother and lecherous brother, and Frasier finds himself forced into playing host. Comedy, guest starring Anthony LaPaglia and Millicent Martin, with Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part two of two. The shrink is unable to stand any more of Daphnes lecherous brother Simon and throws him out, leaving her alone to deal with her conniving mother. Comedy, guest starring Anthony LaPaglia and Millicent Martin, with Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Beaver, Pennsylvania, where he meets a brother-and-sister team who find it almost impossible to work together. Their relationship is so fractured that theyre failing to save their fathers Italian restaurant Levantis from being overwhelmed by local competition, and the situation has become so desperate that their dad is in danger of losing his life savings

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Midday news summary

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to the Chiltern Hills in Buckinghamshire to meet Adam and Mandy, who run a classic car restoration business. Henry uncovers an anvil, while Simon finds an old brown dresser, which he thinks could become a wildlife sanctuary for birds and insects

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers enjoy a trip around Durham Cathedral. Matt visits a nearby quarry to help blast stone used to refurbish the cathedral itself before braving the freezing waters of the River Wear for an underwater treasure hunt. Bird enthusiast Mike gets up close and personal with the owls in the cloisters and meets the craftspeople lovingly conserving the building, while Janice helps to create one of the cathedrals new tapestries

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman jets off to the countryside of Murcia in Spain to help Debra from London, and Greg from Memphis in the USA. The couple are veterans of the West End and Broadway and are looking for a holiday home in the sun which one day may become their permanent home for them and their beloved dog, Trinity. Jasmine shows them five options for their budget of £150,000

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Catching up with Adam and Alicia at their busy cocktail bar on the Costa del Sol, to discover if they have become victims of their own success. Coach tour operator Lesley trials new Spanish culinary experiences to get her business back on the road, and in France, Colin and Kim are finishing up an ambitious gîte renovation

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Kathryn and Steve battle to revive their 14th-century kitchen without losing the buildings sense of history, while Sarah and George face their first wedding of the season

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: With just a month left to renovate the Spanish villa, Jasmine speeds up the build by taking a sledgehammer to the old kitchen walls. A family barbecue on the beach eases the stress, but not for long as theres a problem with the new lounge windows

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: The kennel takes a shot on Bulut, a wary stray Malinois who showed up in the middle of the night. Bailey the cocker spaniel faces the challenge of living up to expectations, while for Moose the staffy, its a chance at second-time luck

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.sub-title: 24 Hours in Police Custody: The Cold Case Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. The inside story of the cold case investigation into the murder of an 86-year-old who was stabbed multiple times in 2013, before her body was set on fire. More than a decade later, detectives from the Major Crime Unit decide to reopen the investigation hoping that advances in DNA science will mean they can finally identify the killer

programme.title: The Disappearance of Jay Slater
programme.desc: Insight into the case of a British teenager who disappeared in Tenerife in the summer of 2024, and how mass online interest set conspiracy theories flying, causing a social media storm led by true-crime keyboard detectives. Featuring never-before-seen CCTV footage, previously unheard audio, the discovery of unsent messages, and access to the Slater family as they search for answers. This documentary is the only media in the world granted full access to the post-mortem, funeral and inquest

programme.title: The Copenhagen Test
programme.desc: When the mission to capture their enemy in Paris goes sideways, Alexander surprises the Orphanage by going off-book and following his own plan. But this time, it could cost him everything. Espionage thriller, starring Simu Liu

programme.title: French Roulette
programme.desc: French mystery, starring Michèle Laroque as a casino manager investigating her daughters disappearance in 1970s Nice, while also facing ruthless competition, poor lower-level management, and Mafia-backed cheats at the roulette table. In French

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to West Nyack, New York, to come to the aid of a family-run Mexican restaurant that is suffering a financial crisis, and a case of hard-headed patriarchy. When the chef arrives he finds himself turfing out the existing customers for their own safety due to a cockroach infestation on the premises

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three sets of contestants from Brighton compete, beginning with Sam and Nick, who produce a simple menu that reflects their busy lives running two businesses and looking after four children. The second couple are Maria and Terry, who offer an Asian-themed meal and an evenings entertainment of writing love poems. Last to host are Dave and his perfectionist girlfriend Sarah, who try to provide a fun-fuelled night full of quality ingredients

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.sub-title: Click to Kill: The AI War Machine
programme.desc: With unique access in Ukraine, with the US army, and from within Israel, Dispatches charts the new technological arms race and asks whether these tools will make warfare more precise and reduce civilian casualties; or will they lead to more wars that are ever more brutal?

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: Reporter Anja Popp is in Nigeria, home to one of the worlds most prolific film industries, to meet the women risking their livelihoods and reputations to push boundaries on screen

programme.title: Kirsties House of Craft
programme.sub-title: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts, Mark-Francis Vandelli and Aggie MacKenzie
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts go head to head as they create iced ombre cakes, and Mark-Francis Vandelli and Aggie MacKenzie battle it out with intricate jelly art

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: Its a familys worst nightmare when two children are taken, Charlie shares some good news with Vicky, and things come to a head in a face-off between two mothers. Also, sparks fly for two villagers and suspicion arises when a resident disappears

programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam lies to Diane about his plans for a weekend away, and Coach tries to break his record for fewest glasses smashed in a month, while Norm starts spending all his time with a new friend, leaving Cliff feeling neglected. American bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Coach is upset about the death of an old baseball buddy and holds a memorial to him in the bar, but then discovers the man once propositioned his wife. Nicholas Colasanto stars

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norm is cajoled into pursuing an attractive new client who seems to be interested in him, but ends up realising how much he appreciates his wife. Comedy, starring George Wendt

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray reluctantly agrees to take Robert out on the town - but makes the mistake of revealing that the evening was not his idea, causing relations between them to nosedive

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tries to teach Ally a lesson by resuming the piano lessons he gave up 25 years earlier - and finds himself wondering why he ever stopped receiving tuition in the first place. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tricks Debra into letting him play golf - but finds it difficult to put a good round together as he contends with a guilty conscience. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: In a bid to boost his popularity with younger listeners, Frasier agrees to appear on KACLs show for teenagers - where the youthful host proceeds to lampoon him mercilessly. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles leaves nothing to chance as he plans the perfect evening to propose to Daphne, but his efforts to persuade Martin to get her meddling mother out of the way by taking her on an impromptu date lead to disaster. Comedy, guest starring Millicent Martin, with John Mahoney and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Liliths supposedly disabled half-brother Blaine Sternin comes to town seeking forgiveness for past misdemeanours - but Frasier is suspicious of his motives. Comedy, guest starring Michael Keaton (Batman)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Martin take pity on Frasier and individually set him up on dates with three women who they believe will sweep him off his feet, but unfortunately things do not go to plan. Comedy, with Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sams Mediterranean Kabob Room in Monrovia, California, and within minutes of his arrival, is caught in the crossfire of an argument between the owner, his wife and their children, all of whom are feeling the pressure of working seven days a week. Can the chef save the business and mend the warring familys frayed relationships?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien are in Lincolnshire, where they meet mother and son Sarah Jane and Josh, who are trying to restore a historic windmill, and Sandy and Andy, who plan to open a glamping site on their family farm

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers head to the Northumberland town of Hexham, home to one of the most scenic racecourses in the country. Matt visits the stables and saddles up for a ride on a winning horse, while Janice gets involved in training dogs for the emergency services before helping to round up the resident sheep from the home straight

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Barbara and Butch from Leicester are searching for the perfect Italian holiday home in Lucca. For their budget of £90,000, they would like a house with two bedrooms thats within walking distance of amenities. Ben Hillman shows them five fantastic options

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Simon and Monica, who own a painting holiday company based in the south of France, expand into a new continent. Financial pressures mount for Amanda and Nick at their guesthouse in southern Spain, while Colin and Helen try to drum up more bookings for their bespoke childrens holidays in Portugal

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: At Château de Pican, Jane and Rory struggle to fit straight skirting boards on centuries-old wonky bathroom floors during their huge gîte renovation. Near Cognac, Bart and Sarah race to turn their home into a fairytale wedding venue for their best friends nuptials, and theyre starting with a nightmare bathroom. In the Charente, new owners Sophie and Ray are determined to transform a boring bedroom into a beautiful boudoir

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: Almost a year into the renovation, the basement starts to take shape and the gym is finished too. To celebrate, the family take a day trip to the Alcazaba fortress in Málaga city

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Keith Brymer Jones and his partner Marj Hogarth have grand plans to transform two dark, damp storerooms into a guest suite leading out to a courtyard, where they hope to build a raw concrete fire trench. Keith softens the space by hand, crafting porcelain night lights, while Marj makes a canvas sail canopy in honour of Pwllhelis maritime heritage

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.sub-title: 24 Hours in Police Custody: The Cold Case Murder
programme.desc: The second of a two-part special looking into the murder of 86-year-old Una Crown, who was stabbed multiple times in 2013. Forensics get a partial DNA match to a man who had previously been arrested but released due to lack of evidence. Police decide to bring him in once more in the hope that after 13 years of denial, he will finally confess to the killing

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service
programme.desc: The no-nonsense chef sets out to help restaurants in need - by spying on them using a state-of-the-art surveillance system and with the help of a mole on the inside. For his first mission, he gains a 360-degree view of Parthenon, a family run Greek restaurant in Washington, DC. It was once a pillar of the community but its decor, utilities and hygiene are severely lacking. Gordon sets out to give owner Pete the tools he needs to improve the eatery so he can hand over the reins to his son

programme.title: The Copenhagen Test
programme.desc: Alexander is on the run from the people who know him best. With Parker hunting him, and his body betraying him, can he stay alive long enough to save those he loves the most? Espionage thriller, starring Simu Liu and Sinclair Daniel

programme.title: The Copenhagen Test
programme.desc: Alexander faces an impossible choice - and the Orphanage must decide if he has betrayed them. Espionage thriller about an intelligence analyst who discovers his brain has been hacked, giving the perpetrators access to everything he sees and hears. Starring Simu Liu

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Married couple Abbie and Charlie join host Anna Richardson and feature as the first picker pair as they search for another woman to join their relationship. Plus 41 year-old actor and glamour puss Chrissie from Leeds is hoping to find husband number four

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: Brendon and Priya want to explore Priyas bisexuality, but it remains to be seen whether her steamy hot tub and sensual time in the bedroom will hit the spot. Chelsea and George disagree over how far they should have taken things in the bedroom with resident couple Kiki and Sophia

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to the upmarket quarter of Hollywood to help the owner of failing health-food restaurant Sante La Brea. As well as struggling to control his disrespectful staff, the proprietor receives a surprise visit from the police at the height of dinner service

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three pairs of partners in Lancashire take part in the dinner party competition, hoping to secure the £1,000 prize. First to host are Rosie and her husband David, who have been married for 25 years and are seriously loved-up. Next, young newlyweds Melisa and Kyle cook together for the first time to mixed reviews. The third and final evening is led by bickering couple Gary and Linda, but the duo may need to work hard if they want to be in with a chance of winning the cash prize

programme.title: French Roulette
programme.desc: French mystery, starring Michèle Laroque as a casino manager investigating her daughters disappearance in 1970s Nice, while also facing ruthless competition, poor lower-level management, and Mafia-backed cheats at the roulette table. In French

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef takes on one of his toughest challenges yet, J Willys Bar and Grille in South Bend, Indiana, which has amassed debts of more than $1million. On finding an inexperienced chef serving food from both the freezer and tins, together with service that reduces one customer to tears, Gordon Ramsay orders an overnight deep-clean of the premises and designs a menu to inspire the kitchen staff

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A dare lands Bart in hospital and he makes up a story that he went to Heaven and met Jesus, prompting Christian film producers to offer the Simpsons a movie deal

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Barts dream girl Mary Spuckler comes to town, but their relationship turns sour when he fails to pay her enough attention. Meanwhile, Homer lands himself in the doghouse after upsetting Marge once too often. With the guest voices of Benedict Cumberbatch and Zooey Deschanel

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart and Milhouse sample the perks of adulthood when a shaving experiment leaves one of the boys looking exactly like his father. Meanwhile, a preoccupied Marge tries to protect Maggie from the dangers of childrens TV

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Wirral on Merseyside compete, beginning with Sandra and her husband Graham who met in the Army more than 17 years earlier. Second to host are travel agent Joanne and her partner Simon who plan to keep their evening uncomplicated, before housewife Helen and banking manager Adam reveal there is more to them than meets the eye

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George heads to Kent to help Pete and Amelie make their huge 16 room Victorian house fit for modern life, with plans to make a playroom and a library. Amelie makes a trip to Shropshire to make her own encaustic tiles in one of the last remaining factories in the country to use traditional methods. Plus, George revisits Michele in Warwickshire to see how she is getting on remodelling her adjoining Grade II listed cottages

Can the chef save the business and mend the warring familys frayed relationships?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien are in Lincolnshire, where they meet mother and son Sarah Jane and Josh, who are trying to restore a historic windmill, and Sandy and Andy, who plan to open a glamping site on their family farm

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers head to the Northumberland town of Hexham, home to one of the most scenic racecourses in the country. Matt visits the stables and saddles up for a ride on a winning horse, while Janice gets involved in training dogs for the emergency services before helping to round up the resident sheep from the home straight

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Barbara and Butch from Leicester are searching for the perfect Italian holiday home in Lucca. For their budget of £90,000, they would like a house with two bedrooms thats within walking distance of amenities. Ben Hillman shows them five fantastic options

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Simon and Monica, who own a painting holiday company based in the south of France, expand into a new continent. Financial pressures mount for Amanda and Nick at their guesthouse in southern Spain, while Colin and Helen try to drum up more bookings for their bespoke childrens holidays in Portugal

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: At Château de Pican, Jane and Rory struggle to fit straight skirting boards on centuries-old wonky bathroom floors during their huge gîte renovation. Near Cognac, Bart and Sarah race to turn their home into a fairytale wedding venue for their best friends nuptials, and theyre starting with a nightmare bathroom. In the Charente, new owners Sophie and Ray are determined to transform a boring bedroom into a beautiful boudoir

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: Almost a year into the renovation, the basement starts to take shape and the gym is finished too. To celebrate, the family take a day trip to the Alcazaba fortress in Málaga city

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Keith Brymer Jones and his partner Marj Hogarth have grand plans to transform two dark, damp storerooms into a guest suite leading out to a courtyard, where they hope to build a raw concrete fire trench. Keith softens the space by hand, crafting porcelain night lights, while Marj makes a canvas sail canopy in honour of Pwllhelis maritime heritage

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.sub-title: 24 Hours in Police Custody: The Cold Case Murder
programme.desc: The second of a two-part special looking into the murder of 86-year-old Una Crown, who was stabbed multiple times in 2013. Forensics get a partial DNA match to a man who had previously been arrested but released due to lack of evidence. Police decide to bring him in once more in the hope that after 13 years of denial, he will finally confess to the killing

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service
programme.desc: The no-nonsense chef sets out to help restaurants in need - by spying on them using a state-of-the-art surveillance system and with the help of a mole on the inside. For his first mission, he gains a 360-degree view of Parthenon, a family run Greek restaurant in Washington, DC. It was once a pillar of the community but its decor, utilities and hygiene are severely lacking. Gordon sets out to give owner Pete the tools he needs to improve the eatery so he can hand over the reins to his son

programme.title: The Copenhagen Test
programme.desc: When the mission to capture their enemy in Paris goes sideways, Alexander surprises the Orphanage by going off-book and following his own plan. But this time, it could cost him everything. Espionage thriller, starring Simu Liu

programme.title: The Copenhagen Test
programme.desc: As Alexander races against the clock, Parker discovers a secret message hiding in plain sight. Espionage thriller, starring Simu Liu, Sinclair Daniel and Melissa Barrera

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: The actor and his son Barney arrive in Texas, where they enjoy driving around in tanks, experience space travel in a centrifuge and hear campfire tales about UFOs.
