programme.desc: Ally has to interview her oldest living relative as part of a school project on genealogy, so Ray helps her track down Franks long-lost Aunt Sarina, who lives in Italy but turns up on the Barones doorstep

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray grows tired of Debras cooking and asks Marie to teach her how to make his favourite dish - spaghetti and meatballs - leading to chaos in the kitchen. Starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Doris Roberts

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray runs into trouble while trying to show Debra he can take over the running of the family finances, and resorts to a series of elaborate lies to get himself out of the mess he has created. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Princess Leia Fantasy
programme.desc: Ross foolishly lets Rachel in on his most secret sexual fantasy, while Phoebe consoles a heartbroken Monica and Chandler gets involved with an old flame whom Joey cannot stand. David Schwimmer and Jennifer Aniston star

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where No Ones Ready
programme.desc: Ross is due to make an important speech at the museum, and invites the gang along for moral support - but bickering and personal problems soon threaten to make him late. Comedy, starring David Schwimmer

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe is alarmed to learn she has attracted the unwanted attentions of a stalker, and Monica comes up with a plan to deal with her broodiness. American comedy, guest starring David Arquette, with Lisa Kudrow and Courteney Cox

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Metaphorical Tunnel
programme.desc: Chandler ponders the importance of commitment, Monica starts work in a restaurant and Phoebes home truths make her an unpopular choice as Joeys agent. Comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc and Matthew Perry

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Actress Isabella Rossellini makes Rosss day by dropping into Central Perk for a coffee. Phoebes efforts to introduce her half-brother to the city give him the wrong idea about her line of work. Comedy, with David Schwimmer, Lisa Kudrow and Giovanni Ribisi

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Janice persuades the pals to reveal their deepest, darkest secrets about a period in their relationships three years earlier. Phoebe is trying to move out without telling Monica, Rachel wants one last fling before she gets married and Ross makes a distressing discovery. Comedy, starring Matthew Perry and Maggie Wheeler

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Race Car Bed
programme.desc: Monica buys a bed from Janices husband but the wrong one is delivered. Joey gets a job teaching a soap opera acting class, while Rachel tries to ease the tension between Ross and her father

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Giant Poking Device
programme.desc: Phoebe convinces herself she is a walking jinx who keeps causing people to die mysteriously. Monica hides the fact that Ben has been injured, while Chandler and Janice make a tough decision about their relationship

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The gang holds an American football match on Thanksgiving. Opposing team captains Ross and Monica relive their sibling rivalry on the pitch, while Joey tries to outflank Chandler

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel hands in her notice at Central Perk, while Ross accidentally breaks a girls leg and ends up having to go door to door peddling cookies. Joey starts a new job selling Christmas trees, but Phoebe thinks it is a cruel trade and sets about saving the merchandise

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Cant Remember Which Sister
programme.desc: Rachels attractive and influential new friend sends Ross into a jealous frenzy, while Chandler gets drunk and falls for the curvaceous charms of one of Joeys many sisters - but later cannot remember which one

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Jealousy
programme.desc: Ross becomes increasingly jealous of Rachels new workmate, and Joey auditions for a Broadway musical - even though he cannot dance. Meanwhile, Monica falls for a would-be poet

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Monica and Richard Are Just Friends
programme.desc: Monica bumps into ex-boyfriend Richard in a video store and the pair agree it would be a mistake to start dating again - but soon fall back into bed. Meanwhile, Phoebe begins seeing an athlete. Tom Selleck guest stars

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebes former singing partner turns up with the idea of using Smelly Cat in a commercial. Chandler dates a woman with a prosthetic leg, and Rosss jealousy gets the better of him. Sherilyn Fenn guest stars

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross and Rachel Take a Break
programme.desc: A harassed Rachel tells Ross she thinks they should take a break from each other - so he responds by getting drunk and sleeping with a woman Joey and Chandler have been chasing. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston and David Schwimmer

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross tries to prevent Rachel discovering he slept with Chloe from the photocopying shop, but she finds out anyway and a major argument ensues at her apartment. Trapped in Monicas bedroom, the other friends cannot help overhearing everything

programme.title: Austin Powers: The Spy Who S*****d Me (1999)
programme.desc: The sex-mad spy is sent back to swinging London at the height of the 1960s in pursuit of the dastardly Dr Evil, who has stolen the secret agents mojo. Teaming up with a female American agent, Austin must stop his arch-rival from causing havoc with a doomsday weapon on the moon. Comedy sequel, starring Mike Myers in multiple roles alongside Heather Graham, Rob Lowe, Robert Wagner, Michael York, Seth Green and Verne Troyer

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: The DIY duo are back for series four! You know the drill: expect funky furnishings, hardware hysteria and, of course, problematic properties. Can Jonathan and Drew reverse more housing screw-ups?A few years ago, Frank and Diana moved into a million-dollar district, but their three-bed fixer-upper is far from fancy. And with four kids and a bird, the walls are closing in... Cue our transformation twins who are ready to help give this family a new start. While Jonathan focuses on reclaiming space, Drew searches for a new nest for them to call home. Short:Frank and Diana live in a million-dollar district, but their fixer-upper is far from fancy. Can the brothers find Frank, Diana, their four kids - and their bird - a new nest to call home?

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Aussies Karla and Aiden begin the contest in south London with a modern menu inspired by their first rendezvous. Later, Delilah and Laurence cook up a romantic Italian feast, while Jane and Johns version of a classic menu sees a catastrophe in the kitchen affect their chances of walking away with the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Newcastle is the location as Rosalind and Roy present an evening of refined dining, Paul and Vanessa focus on their entertainment and Skev and Kayleigh serve up an eclectic menu

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Dele is given £12,000 and three weeks to plan the perfect wedding for his fiancee Tracy. After a 15-year engagement, Tracy is seeking nothing less than her dream of a romantic castle as the venue for her big day, but Dele seems more concerned about arranging a ceremony with a dramatic twist - inspired by his favourite soap opera

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Comic book-loving groom Anthony wants to prove he can be a real superhero to bride-to-be Lauren, by organising a comic-inspired wedding in a cave. However, with Lauren hoping for a traditional affair, Anthony has his work cut out to convince her family that his theme will prove successful

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Jeff Dudan, founder and CEO of environmental services company AdvantaClean, dons a disguise to work incognito among his own staff - including a project manager, a mould-removal technician and a cleaning technician - to ensure his franchise owners are following his template for success

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.sub-title: The Coffee Bean and Tea Leaf
programme.desc: John Fuller, the President and CEO of The Coffee Bean and Tea Leaf, goes undercover in his own firm. Six years earlier, he did the same thing while he was CEO of 1950s diner chain Johnny Rockets. In disguise, he meets a barista, a shift supervisor and a general manager to check just how efficient his coffee shops really are

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Florida to help the owners of Fleming, a Danish restaurant in Miami. A husband-and-wife duo bought the business eight years ago in the hope of continuing the places success, but started losing money rapidly. With a Cuban chef cooking Nordic cuisine and a filthy kitchen, things go from bad to worse until it all comes to a head on relaunch night

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sushi Ko, a Japanese restaurant in California that is losing up to $20,000 a month. Gordon finds owner and chef Akira lacking in confidence and self-esteem as the strain of an empty business is beginning to take its toll on his relationship with this family

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: The DIY duo are back for series four! You know the drill: expect funky furnishings, hardware hysteria and, of course, problematic properties. Can Jonathan and Drew reverse more housing screw-ups?The twins try to turn a leaky bath, outdated features and a purple paint palette into a blank page for Blanca and Teddy. With the plan of netting a top-dollar price on the line, a major makeover snag is not what they need. Can the team solve it, or will the brothers draw a blank? Short:Can the twins turn a leaky bath, outdated features and a purple paint palette into a blank page for Blanca and Teddy?

programme.title: Below Deck

programme.desc: Dan Duffy, the Chief Executive of United Real Estate Group, adopts a disguise to go undercover in his own company. Dan meets many different employees to learn about their professional standards and also their personal lives.

programme.desc: In Hull, image-conscious Jay and Heather kick off the competition with a menu inspired by their love of American food. programme.desc: Groom-to-be Raphael is given £12,000 to plan his wedding to his fiancee Ola, and decides to arrange not one but two ceremonies - a church blessing followed by a large-scale traditional Nigerian celebration. Bride-to-be Ola has been dreaming of a small, intimate occasion, so Raphaels double-feature idea might come as a shock, but if the big day is to get off the ground, the laid-back groom must overcome his habit of leaving everything to the last minute - including the booking of a suitable church

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride

Owner John Esposito left school at the age of 14 and has worked at his parents restaurant, Sals Pizzeria, ever since. programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood look for perfection as they test the eight remaining bakers on three very different types of pastry. For the signature, the cooks are given a Danish-style task, before facing a technical challenge in which they have limited time and instructions to bake a classic British tart. Finally, the contestants create a bite-size showstopper using a fine pastry that is notoriously difficult to make, and even trickier to bake. Also, Mel discovers the history of Baklava, a dessert that originates in the palaces of Sultans

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: In the first ever botanical week, the seven remaining cooks face three tasks inspired by nature as the competition to become Britains best amateur baker continues. Finally, they must create three tiers of elaborately decorated cake for the showstopper, before judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood cast a critical eye over their efforts. programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure

programme.desc: American chef and cookbook writer Ina Garten makes a frozen mocha mousse, easy French apple galettes and balsamic strawberries

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The six remaining bakers battle it out for a place in the quarter-finals. This time, it is dessert week, and Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins assemble the contestants for three more challenges. To begin with, the amateurs face a sweetly filled signature bake, before moving on to prepare a tricky French dessert that proves to be one of the series toughest technical challenges to date. Finally, for the showstopper, Paul Hollywood and Mary Berry ask the bakers to produce multi-bake, mini masterpieces. Plus, Sue visits Paris for a lesson on the history of praline

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The quarter-finals arrive, and only five contestants remain in the tent, and as the stakes get ever higher for the amateur bakers, Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins introduce yet another new Bake Off challenge - Tudor week. All three tasks this week revolve around baking practices from the time of Henry VIII, beginning with a signature bake in the form of savoury-stuffed pies. In the technical heat, the contenders are asked to prepare unusual knotted Tudor biscuits, while the showstopper takes the form of a marzipan spectacle fit to grace a banquet tables at court. Plus, Sue Perkins visits Hampton Court to discover how a sweet delicacy nearly tempted the Virgin Queen Elizabeth I to marry

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The chefs countrywide tour brings him to Exmouth in Devon and a rendezvous with Michael Caines, the owner of Lympstone Manor, a country house hotel and restaurant with stunning views over the River Exe. He gets in the kitchen with Michael to see a Michelin-starred chef at work and then bakes his own lardy cake. James also visits a local vegetable farmer - and cannot resist the chance to drive his tractor - and gathers mussels with fisherman Myles Blood Smyth

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James concludes his culinary trip in Cornwall, where his companion is Paul Ainsworth, who is on hand to taste the award-winning oysters produced by Porthilly Farm. James stops in on his favourite fish supplier, Flying Fish, to see how operations are run and picks up some of todays catch to cook gurnard and red mullet with orange-charred chicory and cauliflower puree

Thriller, starring Tim Roth, Jack OConnell and Talulah Riley

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 68-year-old professional actor visits St Georges Hospital after injuring his shoulder while rushing to catch a train, and a check-up indicates he may need corrective surgery to set things right, but further assessment suggests he might be living with a much more serious condition. An octogenarian couple are rushed in to AandE after being knocked over by a motorcyclist while crossing the road, and the police maintain an active presence as paramedics bring in a man who has collapsed after a suspected drug overdose. Plus, an 88-year-old woman with a swollen leg discusses her ill-fated marriage to a much younger man nearly four decades ago

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: A woman who is 29 weeks pregnant is semi-conscious with a suspected blood clot in her lungs. Dr Scott fears that she could lose her baby and her life. Meanwhile, Dr Luke races against time to save a students arm after a machete attack left a deep wound, and Dr Mya examines an injured mountain biker who took a jump on a country trail and flew eight metres before landing hard

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Peter, 85, has been brought - unwillingly - to AandE by his stepson Brian. He cut his finger on some wire two weeks earlier and his hand is swollen and wont stop bleeding. Emma, 23, is rushed to St Georges after crashing her car on the way to her fathers house, while doctors also see to 86-year-old Harry, who fell down a flight of stairs a day earlier but didnt seek treatment. Ciaran, 27, injured his ankle the previous night while out with friends and was given a temporary cast. Emergency nurse practitioner Helen needs to get him X-rayed and replastered, but he faces a return visit for an operation

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Tree surgeon David, 27, is airlifted to St Georges after falling 40ft to the ground, hitting several branches on his way down and landing head-first onto concrete. Having been knocked unconscious for 10 minutes, the medical team must urgently assess him for internal injuries. Anne, 74, arrives with a sharp pain in her chest, 51-year-old taxi driver Mark receives treatment for a cut on his finger caused by broken glass, and 92-year-old Betty is brought in by ambulance after her carers found her with slurred speech and a shaking hand

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Ending their tour of north Wales in search of the perfect pitch, the seasoned campers arrive at The Oyster Farm Caravan Site on Anglesey. Marine biologist-turned-campsite host Shaun welcomes the campers with freshly caught oysters and his own oyster gin, before sending them on an aquatic tour of the nearby Puffin Island. The weeks winner of the coveted plaque and £2,000 cash prize is then announced

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien visit Gloucestershire to meet Tom, who is obsessed with motocross. Henry upcycles two Porsche wheel rims into a cool pair of stools

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien visit Sussex to meet Bill and Annie, who have a penchant for Star Wars collectibles and most importantly, as far as Henry is concerned, motorbikes

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jon and Julia Shorrock host the first round of the BandB competition from their four-star Derwent House in West Sussex. The guests are split by gender to sample the area, with the men firing machine guns at a shooting range and the women relaxing at a spa - but a carpeted bathroom and shower without hot water raise questions

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Peter and Sharron Sorsky invite their fellow competitors to Kirkby Park BandB in Liverpool, but the presence of a security camera on the outside of the house leaves one guest feeling uneasy. Room inspections uncover all manner of unsightly problems, but a tour of nearby Knowsley Safari Park gives the contestants a chance to enjoy themselves

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Andy and Julian Banner-Price host the competition at their expensive BandB Plas Dinas Country House in Snowdonia, giving their new guests the priciest rooms. Peter and Sharron Sorsky are still reeling from their feedback forms and immediately find fault with the accommodation, and the group is taken to wash bedding the old-fashioned way at the local slate museum

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Annette McAnespie welcomes the group to Hazelwood Farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, for the final BandB visit. The initial impressions are favourable and Peter and Jon begin to bond for the first time during a mosaic class, but not everyone feels up to the planned activity of a cycling tour

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time at Annette McAnespies farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances, before the weeks winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition comes from in and around Brighton, where the first host, 30-year-old medical editor and rapper Adam, serves up a simple yet wholesome and flavoursome vegetarian menu. His guests are 29-year-old copywriter Will, 65-year-old retired advertising sales director Gill, 56-year-old builder Russell and 43-year-old teacher Jo

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Copywriter Will serves up a vegetarian meal on the second evening in Brighton. Theres a seafood surprise over the starter, which leaves teacher Jo with fishy fingers, and Wills risotto is upstaged by a Lady and the Tramp moment between retired sales director Gill and builder Russell. Not one to be outdone, Will gets everyone up to have it large in his living room with a rendition of his own dance track

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Retired advertising sales director Gill hosts the third dinner party of the week in and around Brighton, hoping to impress her guests with a Middle Eastern menu and a fun dress code. Jo, Adam, Will and a newcomer to the group, social worker Ruth, all arrive wearing hats to reflect their personalities, leading to some unexpected revelations and a chance to find out more about newcomer Ruth. Gills cooking impresses, but its not all plain sailing, as theres some honest feedback for her to swallow

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth host in and around Brighton, teacher and surfer Jo, treats her guests to an exotic, tropical-themed evening with a Thai menu. Her guests arrive in their most tropical attire, but after a confident start, with a cheeky massage thrown in for good measure, Jos hit-and-miss food and a dessert drama leave her slightly panicked

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Brighton, the final day of the competition sees social worker Ruth try to impress her guests with her Jewish-Irish fusion menu. Theres chaos in the kitchen before the guests even arrive, then after a mixed-up main and some unexpected veggies, Ruths secret family cheesecake has her guests guessing if it lives up to the bill

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB competition heads to France as four British expats compete, beginning with Lisanne Ashton, who runs the rural Les Chimeres in the south of the country. After searching for truffles in the surrounding area, guest Tony Hortons passion for the royal family causes him to clash with fellow competitor Shaun Baggs, whose mood is worsened by the discovery of bugs in his bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition continues at Le Manoir in the Dordogne, owned by royalist Tony Horton and his Armenian wife Shou Shou - who make a poor first impression by presenting guests with a timetable specifying when they are allowed to use the bathroom. A vinegar-tasting session also receives a tepid response, and some of the visitors are bemused by their hosts turning up for breakfast in costume

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wine-loving Shaun Baggs and his partner Patrick Weaver bring the contest to Le Clos Rabelais in central France, where they invite fellow competitors to take part in a traditional French dance class. Tension mounts as George and Linda become increasingly frustrated with Lisanne, and the bickering continues over dinner when Shaun is confronted about his previous negative feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: George and Linda McGill welcome their guests to the Moulin de la Civiere near Rostrenen in Brittany, where entertainment comes in the shape of a tenpin-bowling outing, and the couple lay on a full English breakfast. However, the hosts receive an unexpected surprise at feedback time courtesy of Lisanne

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the weeks competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go house-hunting in Hertfordshire, with Phil searching for properties for Rich and Bethan, who have lost out on two homes already. Meanwhile, Kirstie helps Chicago-born Lauren and her husband Paul come to terms with the smaller scale of British properties

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer visit the garden city of Milton Keynes to bring harmony to a couple who are worlds apart - both physically and emotionally. Anne-Marie and Paul bought their charming-but-challenging 1920s three-bedroom home six years ago, right before the arrival of their first child. The property was a doer-upper but hasnt been done up yet! Paul wants to list it. Between his twice-a-week 160-mile commute to Manchester, the boxed-in kitchen, and the unforgivable absence of a bathtub in their only bathroom, hes ready to cut his losses and move on. Anne-Marie, on the other hand, refuses to throw in the towel. With family close by and a belief in the houses untapped potential, shes clinging to her dream and counting on Kirstie to work her magic and convince Paul to fall in love with their home again. With a £50,000 Love It budget and an ambitious to-do list, Kirstie gets straight to work, crafting a master plan to unlock every ounce of the houses potential. But just as things get rolling, disaster strikes - a surprise roof repair gobbles up a significant chunk of the budget, leaving Team Love It to rethink their strategy. Meanwhile, Phil takes Anne-Marie and Paul to Rugby, hoping to split the commute burden, and finds jaw-dropping properties with his £500,000 List It budget that leave Anne-Marie questioning her allegiances and Paul well and truly sold

programme.title: Our Farm Next Door: Amanda, Clive and Kids
programme.desc: A second Christmas during the Owens farmhouse renovation is fast approaching, and Amanda and the kids are preparing their farm animals and pets for the cold weather. Richard is keen to get doors fitted so that the house is protected from the elements, while Amanda and her daughter Clemmie meet historian Derek to walk in the footsteps of Anty Johns former occupant, diarist Anthony Clarkson

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Bob, 51, is airlifted to St Georges after falling 20ft from a ladder while repairing his roof. With debris in his wound, doctors are concerned he could develop a life-threatening infection. Retired pharmacologist Anthony, 68, receives help for a chronic gas problem, while 83-year-old Gladys collapsed on the stairs at home and has a dangerously low heart rate

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Zaki, 63, recently underwent a tonsillectomy following a diagnosis of throat cancer, but he has come to AandE after suffering a serious bleed. Doctors try to save 10-month-old baby Amy, who stopped breathing at home, while 21-year-old expectant father Craig appears to have had a miraculous escape after a motocross accident, but has he learnt his lesson?

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Co Durham, Anthony needs specialist attention after he comes off his motorbike. Helicopter medics are diverted from a training mission to help Deyon, who is trapped in his car after a crash on a country road. Meanwhile in Norfolk, theres an urgent call to a cardiac arrest

With debris in his wound, doctors are concerned he could develop a life-threatening infection. programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: A selection of previous investigations from the programme delving into the secrets behind mass-produced food. Matt Tebbutt braves the icy waters of the North Sea to join divers harvesting scallops by hand from the sea bed, and Kate Quilton is in Israel to learn why some limes are much juicier than others

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien visit Gloucestershire to meet Bryan, whos been collecting everything and anything since childhood. Henry upcycles some old oxygen regulators into bedside lamps, while Simon settles for restoring a stylish and traditional wooden dresser

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.desc: The sports car-themed PitStop in Hertfordshire hosts the first round, as owners Melvyn and Sindy Rutter try to make a good impression. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Gill and Peter Tydie invite their fellow competitors to stay at the Victory in Mersea, Essex, a four-star BandB with 26 members of staff to run the bar, restaurant and accommodation. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave Botha hosts at his Butterfly Paragliding BandB on the Isle of Wight. Deborah Morrison and Wayne Bingham are unhappy they must sleep in a tepee in the garden and use an outdoor toilet, and claim they were served watery eggs and rubbery sausages for breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Deborah Morrison and Wayne Bingham host the final night at Braddon Hall in Torquay, Devon, where Melvyn and Sindy take issue with their squeaky four-poster bed. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Edinburgh, Christian radio DJ and gospel singer Eloho, hopes to take her guests to church, gospel-style, with a music-filled evening. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: IT manager and stand-up comic Ryan hosts the third dinner party of the week in Edinburgh, hoping to impress his guests with a menu designed by a friend who is a Michelin Star chef

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Taxi driver Jamie serves up a fine dining menu in Edinburgh but the table chat does not go down well with some and Eloho is unimpressed with the hosts silent-disco strip-tease

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night in Edinburgh, business owner and socialite Maz is determined that a fantastic fondue, a slap-up portion of posh fish and chips and a handsome Highland piper will be enough to see her become the belle of the ball, capturing the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Owner Helen Marriott offers her guest a chance to step off the planet and treats them to essential breathing lessons, a chance to get to know one another in the hot tub, and soul-soothing foot massages at the Fountains Court Holistic Health Hotel in the North Yorkshire resort of Scarborough

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants head to the Whittington and Cat, a pub in Hull that also offers BandB. Proprietor Sue Perkins treats her guests to an afternoon of ice hockey, but they are intrigued by the possible contents of her peculiarly titled breakfast menu

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Khurram Saba hosts the third day of the competition at his Saba Hotel in west London and finds his guests are expecting metropolitan chic, and certainly not fish and chips for dinner. Candida Leaver is extremely displeased when she experiences a sleepless night, while Sue Perkins and Gill take the opportunity to go out and have some fun

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the weeks competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with house-hunters from previous episodes for whom space was a decisive issue. Sarah is ready to list it. But Caradog is determined to love it and is convinced this home can work with Kirsties magic touch. He also loves the garage and thinks itll be hard to beat elsewhere. With a long list of problems to solve and a budget of £75,000, Kirstie has a tricky task on her hands. But will a refurbished family heirloom swing it in her favour? Meanwhile, Phil hits the streets with a search budget of £500,000, looking for three fabulous houses to make Caradog question his allegiance to Team Love It

programme.title: Great Estates from Above

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Pub landlord Tony is brought in with burnt legs as a result of his barbecue gas cylinder exploding - made slightly worse by the fact he was only wearing boxer shorts at the time. Henry, a 93-year-old war veteran, is rushed in with severe breathing difficulties, and builder Bill was knocked unconscious after falling off his shed. His CT scan reveals he has broken his neck in three places, and he may need surgery to avoid damage to his spinal cord

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Keen young rugby player Robert has a dangerously high heart rate and may need to have a pacemaker fitted - a scary thought for the nine-year-olds father. Mark, 32, has fallen 30ft from scaffolding while doing some DIY, and theres concern he may have damaged his spine. Christine, 74, arrives in AandE after an accident in her garden, and shes suffered nasty injuries to her face

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

programme.desc: A selection of favourite investigations, including Jimmy Doherty heading to southern Italy to find out where the flavour comes from in his Earl Grey tea. programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Co Durham, Anthony needs specialist attention after he comes off his motorbike. Helicopter medics are diverted from a training mission to help Deyon, who is trapped in his car after a crash on a country road. Meanwhile in Norfolk, theres an urgent call to a cardiac arrest

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Peter, 85, has been brought - unwillingly - to AandE by his stepson Brian. He cut his finger on some wire two weeks earlier and his hand is swollen and wont stop bleeding. Emma, 23, is rushed to St Georges after crashing her car on the way to her fathers house, while doctors also see to 86-year-old Harry, who fell down a flight of stairs a day earlier but didnt seek treatment. Ciaran, 27, injured his ankle the previous night while out with friends and was given a temporary cast. Emergency nurse practitioner Helen needs to get him X-rayed and replastered, but he faces a return visit for an operation

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Tree surgeon David, 27, is airlifted to St Georges after falling 40ft to the ground, hitting several branches on his way down and landing head-first onto concrete. Having been knocked unconscious for 10 minutes, the medical team must urgently assess him for internal injuries. Anne, 74, arrives with a sharp pain in her chest, 51-year-old taxi driver Mark receives treatment for a cut on his finger caused by broken glass, and 92-year-old Betty is brought in by ambulance after her carers found her with slurred speech and a shaking hand

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Ending their tour of north Wales in search of the perfect pitch, the seasoned campers arrive at The Oyster Farm Caravan Site on Anglesey. Marine biologist-turned-campsite host Shaun welcomes the campers with freshly caught oysters and his own oyster gin, before sending them on an aquatic tour of the nearby Puffin Island. The weeks winner of the coveted plaque and £2,000 cash prize is then announced

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien visit Gloucestershire to meet Tom, who is obsessed with motocross. Henry upcycles two Porsche wheel rims into a cool pair of stools

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien visit Sussex to meet Bill and Annie, who have a penchant for Star Wars collectibles and most importantly, as far as Henry is concerned, motorbikes

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jon and Julia Shorrock host the first round of the BandB competition from their four-star Derwent House in West Sussex. The guests are split by gender to sample the area, with the men firing machine guns at a shooting range and the women relaxing at a spa - but a carpeted bathroom and shower without hot water raise questions

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Peter and Sharron Sorsky invite their fellow competitors to Kirkby Park BandB in Liverpool, but the presence of a security camera on the outside of the house leaves one guest feeling uneasy. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Andy and Julian Banner-Price host the competition at their expensive BandB Plas Dinas Country House in Snowdonia, giving their new guests the priciest rooms. Peter and Sharron Sorsky are still reeling from their feedback forms and immediately find fault with the accommodation, and the group is taken to wash bedding the old-fashioned way at the local slate museum

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time at Annette McAnespies farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances, before the weeks winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition comes from in and around Brighton, where the first host, 30-year-old medical editor and rapper Adam, serves up a simple yet wholesome and flavoursome vegetarian menu. His guests are 29-year-old copywriter Will, 65-year-old retired advertising sales director Gill, 56-year-old builder Russell and 43-year-old teacher Jo

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Copywriter Will serves up a vegetarian meal on the second evening in Brighton. Theres a seafood surprise over the starter, which leaves teacher Jo with fishy fingers, and Wills risotto is upstaged by a Lady and the Tramp moment between retired sales director Gill and builder Russell. Not one to be outdone, Will gets everyone up to have it large in his living room with a rendition of his own dance track

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Retired advertising sales director Gill hosts the third dinner party of the week in and around Brighton, hoping to impress her guests with a Middle Eastern menu and a fun dress code. Jo, Adam, Will and a newcomer to the group, social worker Ruth, all arrive wearing hats to reflect their personalities, leading to some unexpected revelations and a chance to find out more about newcomer Ruth. Gills cooking impresses, but its not all plain sailing, as theres some honest feedback for her to swallow

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth host in and around Brighton, teacher and surfer Jo, treats her guests to an exotic, tropical-themed evening with a Thai menu. Her guests arrive in their most tropical attire, but after a confident start, with a cheeky massage thrown in for good measure, Jos hit-and-miss food and a dessert drama leave her slightly panicked

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Brighton, the final day of the competition sees social worker Ruth try to impress her guests with her Jewish-Irish fusion menu. Theres chaos in the kitchen before the guests even arrive, then after a mixed-up main and some unexpected veggies, Ruths secret family cheesecake has her guests guessing if it lives up to the bill

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB competition heads to France as four British expats compete, beginning with Lisanne Ashton, who runs the rural Les Chimeres in the south of the country. After searching for truffles in the surrounding area, guest Tony Hortons passion for the royal family causes him to clash with fellow competitor Shaun Baggs, whose mood is worsened by the discovery of bugs in his bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition continues at Le Manoir in the Dordogne, owned by royalist Tony Horton and his Armenian wife Shou Shou - who make a poor first impression by presenting guests with a timetable specifying when they are allowed to use the bathroom. A vinegar-tasting session also receives a tepid response, and some of the visitors are bemused by their hosts turning up for breakfast in costume

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wine-loving Shaun Baggs and his partner Patrick Weaver bring the contest to Le Clos Rabelais in central France, where they invite fellow competitors to take part in a traditional French dance class. Tension mounts as George and Linda become increasingly frustrated with Lisanne, and the bickering continues over dinner when Shaun is confronted about his previous negative feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: George and Linda McGill welcome their guests to the Moulin de la Civiere near Rostrenen in Brittany, where entertainment comes in the shape of a tenpin-bowling outing, and the couple lay on a full English breakfast. However, the hosts receive an unexpected surprise at feedback time courtesy of Lisanne

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the weeks competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go house-hunting in Hertfordshire, with Phil searching for properties for Rich and Bethan, who have lost out on two homes already. Meanwhile, Kirstie helps Chicago-born Lauren and her husband Paul come to terms with the smaller scale of British properties

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer visit the garden city of Milton Keynes to bring harmony to a couple who are worlds apart - both physically and emotionally. Anne-Marie and Paul bought their charming-but-challenging 1920s three-bedroom home six years ago, right before the arrival of their first child. The property was a doer-upper but hasnt been done up yet! Paul wants to list it. Between his twice-a-week 160-mile commute to Manchester, the boxed-in kitchen, and the unforgivable absence of a bathtub in their only bathroom, hes ready to cut his losses and move on. Anne-Marie, on the other hand, refuses to throw in the towel. With family close by and a belief in the houses untapped potential, shes clinging to her dream and counting on Kirstie to work her magic and convince Paul to fall in love with their home again. With a £50,000 Love It budget and an ambitious to-do list, Kirstie gets straight to work, crafting a master plan to unlock every ounce of the houses potential. But just as things get rolling, disaster strikes - a surprise roof repair gobbles up a significant chunk of the budget, leaving Team Love It to rethink their strategy. Meanwhile, Phil takes Anne-Marie and Paul to Rugby, hoping to split the commute burden, and finds jaw-dropping properties with his £500,000 List It budget that leave Anne-Marie questioning her allegiances and Paul well and truly sold

programme.title: Our Farm Next Door: Amanda, Clive and Kids
programme.desc: A second Christmas during the Owens farmhouse renovation is fast approaching, and Amanda and the kids are preparing their farm animals and pets for the cold weather. programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE

programme.title: Wonders of the Moon with Dara O Briain
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dara explores the ways in which the moon shapes many things on Earth and why there is a new race to get humans back to its surface. He also talks to Dr Rohin Francis to dig deeper into the myths surrounding the moons influence on human behaviour, and discuss some of the latest evidence

programme.title: A Good Girls Guide to Murder
programme.desc: The disappearance of 17-year-old Andie Bell has haunted the town of Little Kilton for five years, a confessional text pointed the blame at her boyfriend Sal after he took his own life. Now, inquisitive teenage amateur sleuth Pip Fitz-Amobi is determined to clear his name and get to the bottom of what really happened. Crime drama based on the novel by Holly Jackson, starring Emma Myers and Zain Iqbal

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Pembleton investigates a murder, but the case proves to have broader ramifications and it is not long before he finds himself looking into allegations of police brutality. American police drama, starring Andre Braugher, Daniel Baldwin and Ned Beatty

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Tempers flare and tension rises between Pembleton and Gee as they investigate a contentious case involving allegations of police brutality

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: Richard E Grant travels to Venice, said to be the city with the highest concentration of luxury hotels on the planet. He discovers how a measuring error led to the building of the citys biggest swimming pool, tries out a 15,000 euro-a-night royal suite whose bed has been slept in by everyone from the Beckhams to Prince Charles and the Duchess of Cornwall, and visits a hotel that keeps its doors open even when water floods to knee height. He also finds out why breaking a window once earned Ernest Hemingway a 10 per cent discount on his room

programme.title: Harold and the Purple Crayon (2024)
programme.desc: Harold and pals Moose and Porcupine live in a hand-drawn world of limitless possibilities. The trio become concerned when the Old Man, who narrates their illustrated existence, suddenly falls silent. Using his magical crayon, Harold draws a door to the real world and tumbles into the present day as a flesh-and-blood man, who collides head-on with widow Terry and her young son, Mel. Family adventure, starring Zachary Levi, Lil Rel Howery, Zooey Deschanel and Benjamin Bottani

programme.title: Ready Player One (2018)
programme.desc: In 2045, gamers explore a virtual world in the quest for a deceased tycoons fortune. However, they must also contend with an evil corporation who will stop at nothing to get their hands on the prize. Steven Spielbergs fantasy adventure based on Ernest Clines novel, starring Tye Sheridan, Olivia Cooke, Ben Mendelsohn, Simon Pegg and Mark Rylance

programme.title: The Ballad of Wallis Island (2025)
programme.desc: Old tensions resurface when ex-bandmates who were former lovers reunite for a private show at the island home of an eccentric millionaire. Comedy drama, written by and starring Tim Key and Tom Basden, with Carey Mulligan and Sian Clifford

programme.title: My First Summer (2020)
programme.desc: Isolated on a remote property after her mothers death, 16-year-old Claudia finds her world transformed when spirited teenager Grace appears. Drama, starring Markella Kavenagh and Maiah Stewardson

programme.title: Rurangi (2020)
programme.desc: After leaving a decade earlier, a transgender activist returns to the remote community of Rurangi, hoping to reconnect with his father. Drama, starring Elz Carrad and Arlo Green

programme.title: A Nice Indian Boy (2024)
programme.desc: Naveen brings his fiance Jay home to meet his traditional Indian family, and they come to terms with his white-orphan-artist boyfriend while planning the wedding of their dreams. Romantic comedy, starring Karan Soni and Jonathan Groff

programme.title: I, Tonya (2017)
programme.desc: Ice skater Tonya Harding rises through the ranks at the US figure skating championships, but her future in the sport is thrown into question by a shocking incident after her ex-husband steps in. Director Craig Gillespies comedy biopic, starring Margot Robbie, Sebastian Stan, and Allison Janney, who won a Bafta and an Oscar for her performance

programme.title: About a Boy (2002)
programme.desc: An idle womaniser pretends to be a father to meet women in a single mothers group - but he starts to change his selfish ways when a lonely youngster with a depressed mum becomes emotionally attached to him. Romantic comedy adapted from Nick Hornbys novel, starring Hugh Grant, Toni Collette, Nicholas Hoult and Rachel Weisz

programme.title: A Different Man (2024)
programme.desc: After his facial reconstructive surgery, an aspiring actor fixates on a stage production based on his past. Psychological thriller, starring Sebastian Stan, Adam Pearson and Renate Reinsve

programme.title: Anora (2024)
programme.desc: A sex worker marries the son of an oligarch, but his family in Russia try to get the marriage annulled. Writer/director Sean Bakers Oscar-winning romantic comedy, starring Mikey Madison, Paul Weissman and Lindsey Normington

programme.title: May December (2023)
programme.desc: Twenty years after their notorious tabloid romance gripped the nation, a married couple buckles under pressure when an actress arrives to do research for a film about their past. Director Todd Haynes drama, starring Natalie Portman, Chris Tenzis and Julianne Moore

programme.title: The Swimmer (2021)
programme.desc: As he attends an ultra-competitive training camp with an Olympic place up for grabs, an Israeli swimmer starts to fall for one of his competitors. Drama, starring Omer Perelman Striks. In Hebrew with subtitles.

programme.desc: An artists rise to success is scuppered by an unexpected visit from his estranged father, a recovering addict desperate to reconcile. Writer/director Titus Kaphars drama, starring Andre Holland and John Earl Jelks

programme.title: Lost in Translation (2003)
programme.desc: A lonely and past-his-prime American actor travels to Japan to film a commercial. During a depressing night in the hotel bar, he meets a spirited younger woman and the pair strike up a close friendship, exploring Tokyo and helping each other face the mundanities of everyday life. Sofia Coppolas comedy drama, starring Bill Murray, Scarlett Johansson and Giovanni Ribisi

programme.title: A Star Is Born (2018)
programme.desc: Following a gig, fading music star Jackson Maine happens upon Ally, a nightclub singer-songwriter. Their instant chemistry leads to a tempestuous romance as he helps her on the road to stardom. However, Jacksons alcoholism has explosive repercussions as her career blossoms. Oscar-winning drama, starring Lady Gaga, Bradley Cooper, who also directed, and Sam Elliott

programme.title: Promising Young Woman (2020)
programme.desc: Medical-school dropout Cassie is traumatised by a tragic event in her past. Still living with her parents, and waiting tables at a cheap coffee shop to earn a living, she has found the perfect way to deal with the painful past, and seeks vengeance against those who crossed her path. Director Emerald Fennells Oscar-winning crime thriller, starring Carey Mulligan, Bo Burnham and Alison Brie

programme.title: On Swift Horses (2025)
programme.desc: Mild-mannered war veteran Lee Walker plans a life of quiet domesticity in California with his wife Muriel and younger brother Julius. Restless after an early discharge from the US Army, Julius steals money from Lee to seek his fortune in Nevada, where he lands a job in security at a casino and sparks forbidden desire with co-worker Henry, which is consummated away from prying eyes. Back in their hotel room, Julius and Henry fantasise about using the same underhand tactics as card sharps to line their pockets. Drama, starring Daisy Edgar-Jones, Jacob Elordi and Will Poulter

programme.title: Man Finds Tape (2025)
programme.desc: After finding mysterious video clips, siblings investigate the strange recordings and uncover a disturbing secret. Horror thriller, starring Kelsey Pribilski and William Magnuson

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview-April 2026
programme.desc: Previews of the films now playing on Sky Cinema, plus other highlights

programme.title: Blue Moon (2025)
programme.desc: Set over the course of one turbulent night in March 1943, Lorenz Hart, the waspish wordsmith responsible for The Lady Is a Tramp and My Funny Valentine, finds his life unravelling on the opening evening of his ex-partners show, Oklahoma! Director Richard Linklaters biographical comedy drama, starring Ethan Hawke, Margaret Qualley, Bobby Cannavale and Andrew Scott

programme.title: Flavia (2025)
programme.desc: Flavia de Luce, an 11-year-old amateur sleuth, investigates a murder at her familys manor house when her father falls under suspicion. Family adventure, starring Molly Belle Wright, Martin Freeman, Jonathan Pryce and Toby Jones

programme.title: The Bad Guys 2 (2025)
programme.desc: Struggling to adjust to their newly minted lives as Good Guys, the Bad Guys are offered one last job by a group of all-female criminals. Animated sequel, featuring the voices of Sam Rockwell, Marc Maron and Craig Robinson

programme.title: Superman (2025)
programme.desc: Kal-El, one of the last survivors of the planet Krypton, attempts to protect the people of Earth from evil genius Lex Luthor. Superman is assisted by the Justice Gang, comprising Guy Gardner, aka Green Lantern, Kendra Saunders, aka Hawkgirl, Michael Holt, aka Mister Terrific and Rex Mason, aka Metamorpho. Writer/director James Gunns fantasy adventure, starring David Corenswet, Rachel Brosnahan and Nicholas Hoult

programme.title: On Swift Horses (2025)
programme.desc: Mild-mannered war veteran Lee Walker plans a life of quiet domesticity in California with his wife Muriel and younger brother Julius. Restless after an early discharge from the US Army, Julius steals money from Lee to seek his fortune in Nevada, where he lands a job in security at a casino and sparks forbidden desire with co-worker Henry, which is consummated away from prying eyes. Back in their hotel room, Julius and Henry fantasise about using the same underhand tactics as card sharps to line their pockets. Drama, starring Daisy Edgar-Jones, Jacob Elordi and Will Poulter

programme.title: The Bad Guys 2 (2025)
programme.desc: Struggling to adjust to their newly minted lives as Good Guys, the Bad Guys are offered one last job by a group of all-female criminals. Animated sequel, featuring the voices of Sam Rockwell, Marc Maron and Craig Robinson

programme.title: One Battle After Another (2025)
programme.desc: Bob is a washed-up revolutionary who lives in a state of stoned paranoia, with his spirited and self-reliant daughter, Willa. When his evil nemesis resurfaces and Willa goes missing, he scrambles to find her as both battle the consequences of their pasts. Paul Thomas Andersons action thriller loosely based on Thomas Pynchons novel Vineland, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Sean Penn and Benicio del Toro

programme.title: Weapons (2025)
programme.desc: At 2.17am, 17 children from the class of Justine Gandy run into the night, arms outstretched, never to be seen again. The only child from the class who remains safely in their bed is Alex Lilly. Distraught parent Archer Graff joins other members of the local community in pointing a finger of blame at Ms Gandy for the shocking turn of events. While she pleads innocence, school principal Andrew Marcus seeks answers along with police officer Paul Morgan. Horror, starring Julia Garner, Josh Brolin and Alden Ehrenreich

programme.desc: Former baseball player Henry Hank Thompson foolishly agrees to cat-sit for British neighbour Russ, and walks unwittingly into the middle of a turf war between warring factions. Director Darren Aronofskys comedy thriller, starring Austin Butler, Zoe Kravitz and Matt Smith

programme.title: Roofman (2025)
programme.desc: Former US Army Reserve officer Jeffrey Manchester executes a plan to rob multiple branches of McDonalds. He is eventually captured by police and becomes the first person to escape from Brown Creek Correctional Institution in North Carolina. Jeffrey hides out at a toy shop for several months, and during forays outside the building, he sparks romance with store worker Leigh Wainscott. Comedy drama, starring Channing Tatum, Kirsten Dunst and Peter Dinklage

programme.desc: Former assassin Hutch Mansell takes his family on a much-needed holiday, but soon finds himself in the crosshairs of a bloodthirsty crime boss. Comedy thriller, starring Bob Odenkirk, Connie Nielsen and Christopher Lloyd

programme.desc: Born Anna Mae Bullock in 1939, Tina Turner has sold in excess of 100 million records and earned numerous plaudits including Grammy awards and induction into the Rock And Roll Hall Of Fame with her first husband, Ike Turner. She was the first woman to grace the cover of Rolling Stone magazine and dabbled in acting including a memorable performance opposite Mel Gibson in Mad Max Beyond Thunderdome. Following a prolonged period of ill health, Turner intends to retire from the spotlight with her second husband, Erwin Bach. She bids farewell to her fans in the emotional documentary Tina. Here, Raksha Dave, Alex Langlands and assorted volunteers and archaeologists hunt for a lost garden planted as part of the celebrations, while there is also a visit to Kenilworth Castle to investigate an Elizabethan garden still in full bloom today. Plus, a look at how the gardens Tudor owner, Robert Dudley transformed his estate to win favour with his Queen

programme.desc: Stepping into stolen art secrets and uncovering the hidden masterpieces, Altausse mine myths and the obsession behind Hitlers art dream

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.desc: Centuries ago, canals were the liquid highways of Britain, transporting raw materials and finished goods all over the country. Their arrival kick-started the Industrial Revolution, which was to turn Britain into a superpower. The very first water superhighway was the Bridgewater Canal, completed in 1761 to carry coal to Manchester from the mines in Worsley. Here, a team of archaeologists from Salford University roots out this industrial past, working with volunteers to excavate the very coal mine that inspired the creation of the canal

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.desc: Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands search for the remains of a First World War stable at Catterick Garrison in North Yorkshire, where thousands of horses would have been housed and trained for the front line. They join a team comprised of archaeologists and the military attempting to uncover the role this building played in the war effort. They also visit Britains oldest cavalry regiment, examining tools that soldiers would have used to look after their steeds, and hear the story of Warrior, known as the horse the Germans could not kill

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.desc: In 1592, Elizabeth I visited Sudeley Castle in the Cotswolds for a three-day party commemorating the nations victory over the Spanish Armada. Here, Raksha Dave, Alex Langlands and assorted volunteers and archaeologists hunt for a lost garden planted as part of the celebrations, while there is also a visit to Kenilworth Castle to investigate an Elizabethan garden still in full bloom today. Plus, a look at how the gardens Tudor owner, Robert Dudley transformed his estate to win favour with his Queen

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.desc: Archaeologists and presenters Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands join a team from Reading University at a site in Silchester, near Reading, as they examine one of the best-preserved Roman sites in Britain

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The owner of an automotive boneyard is in the mood to sell. A California collectors kingdom of junk proves home to one of the rarest motorbikes in the world

programme.title: Forged in Fire
programme.desc: Four elite bladesmiths are challenged to create the ultimate weapon. The heat is on as they are tasked with crafting a massive, oversized version of a traditional pig hunting spear. This supersized challenge tests their skills and creativity as they must create a spear that is capable of taking down the largest wild boar

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores archaeological treasures in the west of Britain, beginning with a look at the remains of Snodhill Castle, a little-known ruin on the crest of a hill in Herefordshire. Alice also joins a team of volunteers and students from Cardiff University unearthing what they believe to be the oldest house ever found in the city

programme.title: The Last Hunt for Nazi Gold
programme.desc: Stepping into stolen art secrets and uncovering the hidden masterpieces, Altausse mine myths and the obsession behind Hitlers art dream

programme.title: Secret Sex Lives of Tyrants
programme.desc: The sexual power plays, obsessions and abuses that influenced rulers reigns - and sometimes the fate of nations - continuing with Saddam Hussein

programme.title: Hitlers Secret Sex Life
programme.desc: The myriad of rumours, theories and disputed historical accounts of Adolf Hitlers sexual psychology, considering how it integrated into his overall twisted psyche. This series addresses various time periods of Hitlers alleged and proven sex life and explores the role sex played in shaping Hitlers behaviour

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on expeditions through some of Britains most historic landscapes in search of the richest stories from the nations past. He begins with a 40-mile walk along the Derwent Valley in the Peak District, where the worlds industrial revolution was born. En route he visits the sleepy village of Cromford to find out about the work of entrepreneur Richard Arkwright, and stops off at a 900-year-old stately home that has been used as the location for three Jane Eyre films. Brian Blessed also introduces Tony to the joys - and the significance - of the Cromford canal, before this leg of his journey ends in Derby

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson sets off on a 45-mile hike through the countryside of the Weald in Kent and the Downs of East Sussex to discover the areas rich and surprising Tudor heritage. At Penshurst Place, author Philippa Gregory helps him relish the fate of the Grand Duke of Buckingham at the hands of the young Henry VIII. From there, he travels up what used to be secret paths to Hever Castle and finds out how the monarchs reign brought not only huge social, political and industrial change, but also fame and disaster to the women who caught his eye He ends this leg of his journey in Lewes, where he relives one of the more brutal monastic dissolutions

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts joins archaeologists in the North as they uncover writing tablets, swords and domestic items left by Romans at Vindolanda during a time of British rebellion, before finding traces of a long lost monastery and pilgrimage site on the Scottish island of Iona. Meanwhile in the east of Scotland, a weapons hoard belonging to a wealthy Bronze Age warrior is unearthed

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on an expedition along Dorsets Jurassic coast to explore the countys hidden Second World War story. Starting beside the defences on Chesil Beach, he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay. On his five-day walk he encounters stunning scenery and uncovers wartime acts of ingenuity and bravery. He also discovers the crucial part a swannery played in developing a secret weapon and reveals the role of Dorset in protecting Britain from invasion during the conflict

programme.title: Walking Through History

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson goes on a four-day trek through the Kintail region of the north-western Highlands, examining the story of the Jacobite uprisings of the early 1700s along the way. He starts by visiting Iron Age dwellings in Shiel Bridge, and heads to the rebuilt castle on Eilean Donan, the original of which was destroyed after invading Spanish troops landed there and were attacked by British warships. Tony ends in Glen Shiel, the site of a climactic battle in 1719 where royalist troops defeated the rebels

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on a 45-mile walk across Wiltshire, from Avebury to Stonehenge, telling the story of the remarkable development that occurred in the latter days of the Neolithic era. His route over chalk downlands and Salisbury Plain takes him through one of the greatest concentrations of prehistoric sites in Europe

programme.title: Dumb and Dumberer: When Harry Met Lloyd (2003)
programme.desc: The half-witted duo meet for the first time as teenagers and band together to face the perils of high school - and prove they are intelligent enough to leave remedial classes behind. Comedy prequel, starring Eric Christian Olsen, Derek Richardson, Rachel Nichols, Eugene Levy and Mimi Rogers

programme.title: Miss Congeniality (2000)
programme.desc: A feminist FBI agent is sent undercover as a contestant in the Miss United States pageant when the event becomes the target of a terrorist bomb threat. As she is not exactly catwalk material, a veteran British coach is brought on board to oversee her transformation into a glamorous beauty queen. Comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Michael Caine, Benjamin Bratt, Candice Bergen and William Shatner

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview-April 2026
programme.desc: Previews of the films now playing on Sky Cinema, plus other highlights

programme.title: Miss Congeniality 2: Armed and Fabulous (2005)
programme.desc: FBI agent Gracie Hart is taken off active duty and made the face of the bureaus publicity campaign, but would rather catch crooks than be the focus of media attention - so when her friends are kidnapped, she disobeys orders to run her own undercover investigation with the help of a short-tempered colleague. Comedy sequel, starring Sandra Bullock, Regina King, William Shatner and Heather Burns

programme.title: Dumb and Dumber (1994)
programme.desc: Dimwitted Rhode Island limousine driver Lloyd Christmas and his equally vacuous friend Harry Dunne embark on a disaster-fraught cross-country trip to Aspen, where they hope to reunite a briefcase with its female owner. However, the pair are unaware the case contains a $1million ransom they would have been better leaving well alone. Farrelly brothers comedy, starring Jim Carrey, Jeff Daniels, Lauren Holly, Teri Garr and Mike Starr

programme.title: Dumb and Dumberer: When Harry Met Lloyd (2003)
programme.desc: The half-witted duo meet for the first time as teenagers and band together to face the perils of high school - and prove they are intelligent enough to leave remedial classes behind. Comedy prequel, starring Eric Christian Olsen, Derek Richardson, Rachel Nichols, Eugene Levy and Mimi Rogers

programme.title: The Hangover (2009)
programme.desc: Three men on a stag weekend in Las Vegas wake up after a night of heavy drinking and find they have not only misplaced the groom, but somehow gained a tiger and a baby. Unable to remember the details of the night before, they set about piecing the clues together in the hope of finding their missing friend. Comedy, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms and Zach Galifianakis

programme.title: The Hangover Part II (2011)
programme.desc: Phil, Stu and Alan reunite in Bangkok for another stag weekend, but having learned the lessons of their previous misadventures, resolve to keep this one quiet and alcohol-free. Things inevitably do not go according to plan, and they must once again piece together the events of a wild night they cannot remember. Comedy sequel, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms and Zach Galifianakis

programme.title: The Hangover Part III (2013)
programme.desc: Drinking buddies Phil and Stu try to help their troubled friend Alan get his life in order, only to stumble into another bizarre misadventure. The Mob have taken Alans brother hostage and to free him, they have to track down manic gangster Mr Chow, who has escaped from a Thai prison. Comedy sequel, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms, Zach Galifianakis and John Goodman

programme.title: Miss Congeniality (2000)
programme.desc: A feminist FBI agent is sent undercover as a contestant in the Miss United States pageant when the event becomes the target of a terrorist bomb threat. As she is not exactly catwalk material, a veteran British coach is brought on board to oversee her transformation into a glamorous beauty queen. Comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Michael Caine, Benjamin Bratt, Candice Bergen and William Shatner

programme.title: Miss Congeniality 2: Armed and Fabulous (2005)
programme.desc: FBI agent Gracie Hart is taken off active duty and made the face of the bureaus publicity campaign, but would rather catch crooks than be the focus of media attention - so when her friends are kidnapped, she disobeys orders to run her own undercover investigation with the help of a short-tempered colleague. Comedy sequel, starring Sandra Bullock, Regina King, William Shatner and Heather Burns

programme.title: Mamma Mia! (2008)
programme.desc: A former member of a singing trio, now running a hotel on a Greek island, is reunited with her old bandmates for her daughters wedding - but is surprised when three of her old flames also show up unexpectedly. The three men have been invited by the bride, who wants to find out which one of them is her father. Musical comedy, with a soundtrack of Abba songs, starring Meryl Streep, Pierce Brosnan, Julie Walters, Colin Firth, Stellan Skarsgard and Amanda Seyfried

programme.title: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again (2018)
programme.desc: Its five years since Sophie invited her three possible dads to her wedding, and now most of her family are reunited as she prepares for the grand reopening of the Hotel Bella Donna. Along the way, she learns more about her mothers past. Abba-themed musical sequel, starring Amanda Seyfried, Lily James, Pierce Brosnan, Cher and Andy Garcia

programme.title: Ace Ventura: Pet Detective (1994)
programme.desc: An eccentric but brilliant former cop sets himself up to champion the underdog - as a private eye specialising in locating missing animals. The zany detective is called in to find a kidnapped dolphin - the mascot of an American football team - and uncovers a plot involving high-level police corruption. Comedy, starring Jim Carrey, Courteney Cox, Sean Young, Tone Loc and Noble Willingham

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop (1984)
programme.desc: Fast-talking Detroit cop Axel Foley heads for Los Angeles after an old friend is murdered before his eyes. His search for the killers leads him into conflict with a seemingly untouchable drug kingpin, but he also has to contend with the local force, who do not want him taking matters into his own hands. Comedy thriller, starring Eddie Murphy, Judge Reinhold, Ronny Cox, John Ashton and Steven Berkoff

programme.title: Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls (1995)
programme.desc: The eccentric pet detective is coaxed out of self-imposed exile in a Buddhist monastery to visit Africa and help search for a sacred white bat. However, his unique investigative talents are tested to the limits as he faces a race against time to find the missing creature and prevent a tribal war. Comedy sequel, starring Jim Carrey, Simon Callow and Bob Gunton

programme.title: Look Whos Talking (1989)
programme.desc: Pregnant Mollie is dumped by her boyfriend, and sets out after the babys birth to find a surrogate father for her infant son Mikey, who comments on proceedings through a wisecracking inner monologue. Mikey is confident the cab driver who helped deliver him is the perfect man for the job - but his mother is less than convinced. Comedy, with John Travolta, Kirstie Alley and the voice of Bruce Willis

programme.title: Look Whos Talking Too (1990)
programme.desc: Wisecracking toddler Mikey is joined by mischievous baby sister Julie and sibling rivalry inevitably erupts - while their parents have problems of their own to deal with, in the shape of the kids good-for-nothing uncle. Comedy sequel, starring John Travolta and Kirstie Alley, with the voices of Bruce Willis and Rosanne Barr

programme.title: Look Whos Talking Now (1993)
programme.desc: A family takes in two pet dogs, a scruffy mongrel with discipline problems and a prim and proper pedigree poodle, whose personality clashes create all sorts of mayhem for the household. Comedy sequel, starring John Travolta and Kirstie Alley, plus the voices of Diane Keaton and Danny DeVito as the canine duo

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop (1984)
programme.desc: Fast-talking Detroit cop Axel Foley heads for Los Angeles after an old friend is murdered before his eyes. His search for the killers leads him into conflict with a seemingly untouchable drug kingpin, but he also has to contend with the local force, who do not want him taking matters into his own hands. Comedy thriller, starring Eddie Murphy, Judge Reinhold, Ronny Cox, John Ashton and Steven Berkoff

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop II (1987)
programme.desc: Wisecracking Detroit detective Axel Foley returns to Los Angeles to investigate the shooting of an old friend, and uncovers a connection with a spate of violent robberies carried out by a criminal who leaves a mysterious calling card. Comedy thriller sequel, starring Eddie Murphy, Judge Reinhold, John Ashton, Ronny Cox and Brigitte Nielsen.

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop III (1994)
programme.desc: Maverick cop Axel Foley returns to Los Angeles when an investigation into a colleagues murder leads him to a Hollywood theme park. He enlists the help of old ally Rosewood to prove the resort is a cover for a major counterfeiting ring connected to a terrorist conspiracy. Comedy adventure sequel, starring Eddie Murphy, Judge Reinhold, Theresa Randle and Bronson Pinchot.

programme.title: Mamma Mia! (2008)
programme.desc: A former member of a singing trio, now running a hotel on a Greek island, is reunited with her old bandmates for her daughters wedding - but is surprised when three of her old flames also show up unexpectedly. The three men have been invited by the bride, who wants to find out which one of them is her father. Musical comedy, with a soundtrack of Abba songs, starring Meryl Streep, Pierce Brosnan, Julie Walters, Colin Firth, Stellan Skarsgard and Amanda Seyfried

programme.title: Ace Ventura: Pet Detective (1994)
programme.desc: An eccentric but brilliant former cop sets himself up to champion the underdog - as a private eye specialising in locating missing animals. The zany detective is called in to find a kidnapped dolphin - the mascot of an American football team - and uncovers a plot involving high-level police corruption. Comedy, starring Jim Carrey, Courteney Cox, Sean Young, Tone Loc and Noble Willingham

programme.title: Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls (1995)
programme.desc: The eccentric pet detective is coaxed out of self-imposed exile in a Buddhist monastery to visit Africa and help search for a sacred white bat. However, his unique investigative talents are tested to the limits as he faces a race against time to find the missing creature and prevent a tribal war. Comedy sequel, starring Jim Carrey, Simon Callow and Bob Gunton

programme.title: Bridget Joness Diary (2001)
programme.desc: A single woman begins a new year with resolutions to find love, quit her bad habits and take a step up the career ladder. Her plans crumble as she tries to deal with her mothers infidelity, get over a passionate fling with her boss and come to terms with her feelings for an uptight lawyer. Romantic comedy based on Helen Fieldings novel, starring Renee Zellweger, Hugh Grant and Colin Firth

programme.title: Bridget Jones: The Edge of Reason (2004)
programme.desc: The disaster-prone journalist seems to have finally found the perfect boyfriend. However, when the couple start to squabble over their personal habits, she decides to decamp for a while to Thailand and write a travelogue. Unfortunately, she has reckoned without the reappearance of a duplicitous old flame - and a spell behind bars in a Thai prison. Romantic comedy sequel, with Renee Zellweger, Colin Firth, Hugh Grant, Jim Broadbent and Gemma Jones

programme.title: Bridget Joness Baby (2016)
programme.desc: Bridgets focus on single life and her career is interrupted when she finds herself pregnant, but with one hitch - she can only be 50 per cent sure of the identity of her babys father after a pair of one-night stands. Is the dad old flame Mark Darcy or US dating guru Jack Qwant? Comedy sequel, starring Renee Zellweger, Colin Firth, Patrick Dempsey, Emma Thompson and Sally Phillips

programme.title: The Karate Kid (1984)
programme.desc: A teenager at a new school befriends a pretty girl, but her ex-boyfriend is jealous and attacks him. During one particularly bad fight, a wise old caretaker intervenes and defeats the youngsters assailants using his martial arts skills. He goes on to mentor the boy and teach him karate so he can face his enemies in a tournament. Drama, with Ralph Macchio, Pat Morita and Elisabeth Shue

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop II (1987)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Swindon, Catherine is recovering from multiple brain surgeries and wants a garden in which to relax and recuperate. She would like its design to reflect the period character of her Victorian terraced property and has a budget of £3,000 to achieve this. Designers Chris Hull and Lee Burkhill rise to the challenge, with both having peace and calm at the core of their proposed schemes

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Flo Headlam and Chris Hull head to the cathedral city of Wells, Somerset, to address the garden of David and Fiona. The pair transform the small plot into a colourful garden with a Moroccan influence

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team design a garden of peace and tranquillity for Bluebell Wood Childrens Hospice in Sheffield. Alans vision is a riverbed setting and a visit to picturesque Lathkill Dale in the Peak District provides plenty of inspiration. As the experts struggle against the elements, they hope to create an area where families can gather to remember their loved ones

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Stories
programme.desc: Rick spends a second day in London, where brewer Jaega Wise explains why craft beer has become so fashionable. He also learns about Walthamstows silent film studios, and in west London, over a bowl of borscht at a Ukrainian restaurant staffed by refugees, he is reminded of how food creates community

programme.title: Robson Greens Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: The actor is joined by his Uncle Matheson to test a night in a local youth hostel. They sign up for a cookery class, take a guided walk on Hadrians Wall and visit remote honey bee hives
